```{python}
                RUN_DIR / "SMOKE_RUN_DO_NOT_USE.txt",
                b"This is a smoke test run. Do not use as production/latest.\n"
            )

        # --------------------------------------------
        # Decide if allowed to touch GLOBAL
        # --------------------------------------------
        TOUCH_GLOBAL = (TRAIN_MODE == "full")

        # Run-scoped stage files
        s1 = _stage_paths(RUN_DIR, "stage1")
        s2 = _stage_paths(RUN_DIR, "stage2")
        sf = _stage_paths(RUN_DIR, "final")

        # -------------------------
        # Stage 1: coarse tuning
        # -------------------------
        # Preference order for FULL mode:
        #   (a) use global stage1 params if done and not retraining
        #   (b) otherwise run stage1 and save to RUN_DIR (and global if FULL)
        if TOUCH_GLOBAL and STAGE1_DONE.exists() and STAGE1_PARAMS_PATH.exists() and (not RETRAIN_CATBOOST):
            print("✅ Stage 1 already done (GLOBAL) — loading best params.")
            best1 = json.loads(STAGE1_PARAMS_PATH.read_text())
            # also write into run folder for auditability
            atomic_write_json(s1["params"], best1)
            mark_done(s1["done"], "ok (loaded global)")
        else:
            print("🧪 Stage 1: coarse tuning")
            mark_in_progress(s1["inprog"], "stage1 started")

            if TOUCH_GLOBAL:
                mark_in_progress(STAGE1_INPROGRESS, "stage1 started (global)")

            try:
                cv1 = KFold(n_splits=cv1_splits, shuffle=True, random_state=42)

                cb_base = CatBoostRegressor(
                    loss_function="RMSE",
                    random_seed=42,
                    verbose=0,
                    allow_writing_files=False
                )

                gs1 = GridSearchCV(
                    estimator=cb_base,
                    param_grid=param_grid_1,
                    scoring="neg_root_mean_squared_error",
                    cv=cv1,
                    n_jobs=1,   
                    verbose=2
                )

                gs1.fit(X_train_cb, y_train_cb, cat_features=cat_cols_cb)

                best1 = gs1.best_params_
                print("Stage 1 best params:", best1)
                print("Stage 1 best CV RMSE (log):", -gs1.best_score_)

                # Always save run-scoped
                atomic_write_json(s1["params"], best1)
                mark_done(s1["done"])

                # Only FULL updates global artifacts
                if TOUCH_GLOBAL:
                    atomic_write_json(STAGE1_PARAMS_PATH, best1)
                    mark_done(STAGE1_DONE)

            finally:
                clear_file(s1["inprog"])
                if TOUCH_GLOBAL:
                    clear_file(STAGE1_INPROGRESS)

        # -------------------------
        # Stage 2: refinement
        # -------------------------
        if TOUCH_GLOBAL and STAGE2_DONE.exists() and STAGE2_PARAMS_PATH.exists() and (not RETRAIN_CATBOOST):
            print("✅ Stage 2 already done (GLOBAL) — loading refined params.")
            best2 = json.loads(STAGE2_PARAMS_PATH.read_text())
            atomic_write_json(s2["params"], best2)
            mark_done(s2["done"], "ok (loaded global)")
        else:
            print("🧪 Stage 2: refinement")
            mark_in_progress(s2["inprog"], "stage2 started")

            if TOUCH_GLOBAL:
                mark_in_progress(STAGE2_INPROGRESS, "stage2 started (global)")

            try:
                cv2 = KFold(n_splits=cv2_splits, shuffle=True, random_state=42)

                stage2_grid = {
                    "depth": [best1["depth"]],
                    "learning_rate": [best1["learning_rate"]],
                    "l2_leaf_reg": [best1["l2_leaf_reg"]],
                    **param_grid_2
                }

                cb_base2 = CatBoostRegressor(
                    loss_function="RMSE",
                    random_seed=42,
                    verbose=0,
                    allow_writing_files=False
                )

                gs2 = GridSearchCV(
                    estimator=cb_base2,
                    param_grid=stage2_grid,
                    scoring="neg_root_mean_squared_error",
                    cv=cv2,
                    n_jobs=1,
                    verbose=2
                )

                gs2.fit(X_train_cb, y_train_cb, cat_features=cat_cols_cb)

                best2 = gs2.best_params_
                best_cb = gs2.best_estimator_

                print("Stage 2 best params:", best2)
                print("Stage 2 best CV RMSE (log):", -gs2.best_score_)

                y_pred_log = best_cb.predict(X_val_cb)
                y_pred = np.expm1(y_pred_log)
                y_true = np.expm1(y_val_cb)

                mae_cv = mean_absolute_error(y_true, y_pred)
                rmse_cv = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred))

                print(f"CV-BEST VAL MAE ($):  {mae_cv:,.0f}")
                print(f"CV-BEST VAL RMSE ($): {rmse_cv:,.0f}")

                atomic_write_json(s2["params"], best2)
                mark_done(s2["done"])

                if TOUCH_GLOBAL:
                    atomic_write_json(STAGE2_PARAMS_PATH, best2)
                    mark_done(STAGE2_DONE)

            finally:
                clear_file(s2["inprog"])
                if TOUCH_GLOBAL:
                    clear_file(STAGE2_INPROGRESS)

        # -------------------------
        # Stage 3: final refit + early stopping on VAL
        # -------------------------
        print("🏁 Stage 3: final refit with early stopping (VAL)")
        mark_in_progress(sf["inprog"], "final refit started")

        if TOUCH_GLOBAL:
            mark_in_progress(FINAL_INPROGRESS, "final refit started (global)")

        try:
            final_params = best2.copy()
            final_params["iterations"] = final_iterations  # cap; early stopping decides

            final_cb = CatBoostRegressor(
                **final_params,
                loss_function="RMSE",
                random_seed=42,
                verbose=200,
                allow_writing_files=False
            )

            final_cb.fit(
                X_train_cb, y_train_cb,
                cat_features=cat_cols_cb,
                eval_set=(X_val_cb, y_val_cb),         
                early_stopping_rounds=early_stop,
                use_best_model=True
            )

            assert final_cb.is_fitted(), "CatBoost model not fitted; refusing to save."
            
            print("Best iteration:", final_cb.get_best_iteration())

            # Save feature columns
            atomic_write_json(RUN_DIR / "feature_columns.json", list(X_train_cb.columns))
            if TOUCH_GLOBAL:
                atomic_write_json(FEATURE_COLS_PATH, list(X_train_cb.columns))

            # Save params
            atomic_write_json(sf["params"], final_params)           
            atomic_write_json(RUN_DIR / "final_params.json", final_params)  
            if TOUCH_GLOBAL:
                atomic_write_json(FINAL_PARAMS_PATH, final_params)

            # Save model versioned always
            versioned_model_path = RUN_DIR / "catboost_final.cbm"
            final_cb.save_model(str(versioned_model_path))

            # Only FULL updates "latest"
            if should_update_latest(TRAIN_MODE):
                final_cb.save_model(str(CATBOOST_FINAL_PATH))
                print("✅ Updated latest:", CATBOOST_FINAL_PATH)
            else:
                print("🚫 Smoke run: NOT updating latest pointer.")

            mark_done(sf["done"])

            if TOUCH_GLOBAL:
                mark_done(FINAL_DONE)

            print("💾 Saved CatBoost model:")
            print("  - Latest:", CATBOOST_FINAL_PATH)
            print("  - Versioned:", versioned_model_path)
            print("  - Run folder:", RUN_DIR)

        finally:
            clear_file(sf["inprog"])
            if TOUCH_GLOBAL:
                clear_file(FINAL_INPROGRESS)
```

#### Catboost Evaluation
```{python}
# ==========================================================
# CATBOOST EVALUATION
# ==========================================================
if final_cb is None:
    print("⚠️ CatBoost model not available — skipping evaluation.")
else:
    # ----------------------------
    # TRAIN split metrics
    # ----------------------------
    cb_pred_train = np.expm1(final_cb.predict(X_train))
    cb_true_train = np.expm1(y_train)

    cb_tr = metrics_full_and_clipped(cb_true_train, cb_pred_train, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99)
    display(metrics_to_df(cb_tr, "CatBoost (Train)"))

    # ----------------------------
    # VAL split metrics (early-stopping set)
    # ----------------------------
    cb_pred_val = np.expm1(final_cb.predict(X_val))
    cb_true_val = np.expm1(y_val)

    cb_v = metrics_full_and_clipped(cb_true_val, cb_pred_val, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99)
    display(metrics_to_df(cb_v, "CatBoost (Val)"))

    # ----------------------------
    # TEST split metrics
    # ----------------------------
    cb_pred_test = np.expm1(final_cb.predict(X_test))
    cb_true_test = np.expm1(y_test)

    print("Best iteration:", final_cb.get_best_iteration())

    cb_m = metrics_full_and_clipped(cb_true_test, cb_pred_test, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99)
    print(f"CATBOOST TEST RMSE ($): {cb_m['full']['rmse']:,.0f}")

    display(metrics_to_df(cb_m, "CatBoost (Test)"))

    # ----------------------------
    # Train+Val metrics for comparison
    # ----------------------------
    X_trainval_cb = pd.concat([X_train, X_val], axis=0)
    y_trainval_cb = pd.concat([y_train, y_val], axis=0)

    cb_pred_trainval = np.expm1(final_cb.predict(X_trainval_cb))
    cb_true_trainval = np.expm1(y_trainval_cb)

    cb_tv = metrics_full_and_clipped(cb_true_trainval, cb_pred_trainval, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99)
    display(metrics_to_df(cb_tv, "CatBoost (Train+Val)"))

    # ----------------------------
    # Feature Importance
    # ----------------------------
    importances = final_cb.get_feature_importance()
    fi = (
        pd.DataFrame({"feature": X_train.columns, "importance": importances})
        .sort_values("importance", ascending=False)
    )
    display(fi.head(25))

    # Globals for metrics writer (clipped + full)
    cb_test_full = cb_m["full"]
    cb_test_clipped = cb_m["clipped"]

    cb_trainval_full = cb_tv["full"]
    cb_trainval_clipped = cb_tv["clipped"]

    cb_train_full = cb_tr["full"]
    cb_train_clipped = cb_tr["clipped"]

    cb_val_full = cb_v["full"]
    cb_val_clipped = cb_v["clipped"]
```

### Keras Neural Network

#### Keras Setup
```{python}
# ----------------------------
# Step 0: Reproducibility
# ----------------------------
SEED = 42
tf.keras.utils.set_random_seed(SEED)
np.random.seed(SEED)
```

```{python}
# ----------------------------
# Step -1: Hyperparameters in one place (tune here)
# ----------------------------
KERAS_TUNE = {
    # --- training modes ---
    "batch_full": 2048,
    "epochs_full": 120,     # EarlyStopping determines actual stop

    # --- callbacks ---
    "es_patience_full": 10,
    "es_patience_smoke": 3,
    "rlrop_patience_full": 3,
    "rlrop_patience_smoke": 1,
    "rlrop_factor": 0.5,
    "min_lr": 1e-6,

    # --- evaluation/predict ---
    "pred_batch": 4096,

    # --- tuner (coarse) Hyperband ---
    "hb_max_epochs_full": 60,
    "hb_max_epochs_smoke": 12,
    "hb_factor": 3,
    "hb_executions_per_trial": 1,

    # --- tuner (fine) Bayesian ---
    "bayes_epochs_full": 60,
    "bayes_epochs_smoke": 8,
    "bayes_max_trials_full": 25,
    "bayes_max_trials_smoke": 10,
    "bayes_executions_per_trial": 1,

    # --- smoke speed controls ---
    "batch_smoke": 512,
    "epochs_smoke": 10,
    "smoke_subsample": True,
    "smoke_train_n": 15000,
    "smoke_val_n": 5000,

    # --- base defaults if you run WITHOUT tuner (fallback) ---
    "fallback_learning_rate": 3e-4,
    "fallback_loss": "huber",        # "mse" | "mae" | "huber"
    "fallback_huber_delta": 0.5,
    "fallback_activation": "silu",   # "silu" | "gelu"
    "fallback_use_residuals": True,
}
```

```{python}
# ----------------------------
# Step 1: Define feature columns 
# ----------------------------

# Keep original globals untouched
NUM_COLS_BASE = list(num_cols)
CAT_COLS_BASE = list(cat_cols)

# Keras-specific copies 
num_cols_nn = list(NUM_COLS_BASE)
cat_cols_nn = list(CAT_COLS_BASE)

print("Numeric cols (base):", len(NUM_COLS_BASE))
print("Categorical cols (base):", len(CAT_COLS_BASE))

# ----------------------------
# Step 2: Prepare train/val/test (NN copies)
# ----------------------------
X_train_nn = X_train.copy()
X_val_nn   = X_val.copy()
X_test_nn  = X_test.copy()

# Add missing indicators
missing_indicator_cols = []
for col in ["engine_liters", "engine_cylinders"]:
    if col in X_train_nn.columns:
        miss = f"{col}_missing"
        for df_ in (X_train_nn, X_val_nn, X_test_nn):
            df_[miss] = df_[col].isna().astype("int64")
        missing_indicator_cols.append(miss)

# Update ONLY the NN numeric list
for miss in missing_indicator_cols:
    if miss not in num_cols_nn:
        num_cols_nn.append(miss)

# numeric impute using TRAIN medians
train_medians = X_train_nn[num_cols_nn].median(numeric_only=True)
X_train_nn[num_cols_nn] = X_train_nn[num_cols_nn].fillna(train_medians)
X_val_nn[num_cols_nn]   = X_val_nn[num_cols_nn].fillna(train_medians)
X_test_nn[num_cols_nn]  = X_test_nn[num_cols_nn].fillna(train_medians)

# categorical fill/cast
for c in cat_cols_nn:
    X_train_nn[c] = X_train_nn[c].fillna("unknown").astype(str)
    X_val_nn[c]   = X_val_nn[c].fillna("unknown").astype(str)
    X_test_nn[c]  = X_test_nn[c].fillna("unknown").astype(str)

# targets (already log1p)
y_train_nn = y_train.to_numpy().astype("float32").reshape(-1, 1)
y_val_nn   = y_val.to_numpy().astype("float32").reshape(-1, 1)
y_test_nn  = y_test.to_numpy().astype("float32").reshape(-1, 1)

print("Rows:", {"train": len(X_train_nn), "val": len(X_val_nn), "test": len(X_test_nn)})
print("NN numeric cols:", len(num_cols_nn), "| NN cat cols:", len(cat_cols_nn))

# DEBUG

# Compute output bias initializer from training data mean
# Must be defined before build_model_from_hp is called
LOG1P_MEAN = float(y_train_nn.mean())  # y_train_nn is already log1p-transformed
print(f"LOG1P_MEAN (output bias init): {LOG1P_MEAN:.4f}")
```

#### Keras Model Preprossesing 
```{python}
# ----------------------------
# Step 3: Build preprocessing layers
# - Normalization for numeric
# - StringLookup + Embedding per categorical feature
# ----------------------------

latest_path = KERAS_LATEST_PATH if "KERAS_LATEST_PATH" in globals() else (MODEL_DIR / "keras" / "keras_model.keras")

WILL_TRAIN_KERAS = (
    (TRAIN_MODE == "smoke") or
    (TRAIN_MODE == "full" and (RETRAIN_KERAS or (not latest_path.exists())))
)

print("Keras WILL_TRAIN_KERAS =", WILL_TRAIN_KERAS, "| latest exists =", latest_path.exists())

num_norm = None
lookups = None
embed_dims = None
vocab_sizes = None

def suggest_embed_dim(vocab_size: int) -> int:
    dim = int(np.ceil(np.log2(vocab_size + 1) * 2))
    return int(np.clip(dim, 8, 50))

if not WILL_TRAIN_KERAS:
    print("⏭️ Skipping adapt/build preprocessing layers (will load existing Keras model later).")
else:
    num_norm = layers.Normalization(name="num_norm")
    num_norm.adapt(X_train_nn[num_cols_nn].astype("float32").to_numpy())

    lookups = {}
    embed_dims = {}
    vocab_sizes = {}

    for c in cat_cols_nn:
        lookup = layers.StringLookup(
            output_mode="int",
            mask_token=None,
            num_oov_indices=1,
            name=f"{c}_lookup"
        )
        lookup.adapt(X_train_nn[c].astype(str).to_numpy())

        vocab_size = int(lookup.vocabulary_size())  # includes OOV bucket
        dim = suggest_embed_dim(vocab_size)

        lookups[c] = lookup
        vocab_sizes[c] = vocab_size
        embed_dims[c] = int(dim)

    print("Embedding plan:")
    for c in cat_cols_nn:
        print(f"  {c:20s} vocab={vocab_sizes[c]:6d}  emb_dim={embed_dims[c]:2d}")

    # ----------------------------
    # Freeze preprocessing state for tuner stability
    # (so each trial gets fresh layer instances)
    # ----------------------------
    NUM_NORM_WEIGHTS = num_norm.get_weights()

    LOOKUP_VOCABS = {}
    for c in cat_cols_nn:
        lk = lookups[c]
        try:
            vocab = lk.get_vocabulary(include_special_tokens=True)
        except TypeError:
            vocab = lk.get_vocabulary()
        LOOKUP_VOCABS[c] = vocab

    # Recompute vocab_sizes from frozen vocabs
    FROZEN_VOCAB_SIZES = {c: len(LOOKUP_VOCABS[c]) for c in cat_cols_nn}

    print("✅ Frozen preprocessing:")
    print("  num_norm weights:", [w.shape for w in NUM_NORM_WEIGHTS])
    print("  lookup vocab sizes:", {c: FROZEN_VOCAB_SIZES[c] for c in cat_cols_nn})

```

#### Keras Model Building 
```{python}
# ----------------------------
# Step 4: Convert pandas -> Keras-friendly input dicts
# ----------------------------
# def to_input_dict(Xdf: pd.DataFrame):
#     d = {"numeric": Xdf[num_cols_nn].to_numpy(dtype=np.float32, copy=True)}
#     for c in cat_cols_nn:
#         d[c] = Xdf[c].astype(str).to_numpy(copy=True).reshape(-1, 1)
#     return d

# train_inputs = to_input_dict(X_train_nn)
# val_inputs   = to_input_dict(X_val_nn)
# test_inputs  = to_input_dict(X_test_nn)

# ----------------------------
# Helper: pandas -> Keras input dict with SAFE dtypes
# (avoids "Invalid dtype: object")
# ----------------------------
def to_input_dict(Xdf: pd.DataFrame) -> dict:
    out = {}
    out["numeric"] = Xdf[num_cols_nn].to_numpy(dtype=np.float32, copy=True)

    for c in cat_cols_nn:
        # Keep as Python strings (object) and let to_tf_inputs convert cleanly to tf.string
        out[c] = Xdf[c].fillna("unknown").astype(str).to_numpy(copy=False)

    return out

# Build inputs (NOW safe)
train_inputs = to_input_dict(X_train_nn)
val_inputs   = to_input_dict(X_val_nn)
test_inputs  = to_input_dict(X_test_nn)

# ----------------------------
# Convert numpy inputs -> TF tensors with supported dtypes
# (fixes Keras 3 / optree "Invalid dtype: str###")
# ----------------------------
def to_tf_inputs(inputs_np: dict) -> dict:
    out = {}
    for k, v in inputs_np.items():
        if k == "numeric":
            out[k] = tf.convert_to_tensor(v, dtype=tf.float32)
        else:
            # Ensure a 1D tensor of tf.string
            # (avoid numpy unicode dtype like <U544 / "str544")
            out[k] = tf.convert_to_tensor(v.tolist(), dtype=tf.string)
    return out

# Build TF versions (use THESE for ds + predict)
train_inputs_tf = to_tf_inputs(train_inputs)
val_inputs_tf   = to_tf_inputs(val_inputs)
test_inputs_tf  = to_tf_inputs(test_inputs)


# ----------------------------
# Step 5: Model builder (HP-aware) + Tuner integration
#   - Build fresh preprocessing layers per trial to avoid KerasTuner state reuse issues.
#   - Uses frozen NUM_NORM_WEIGHTS + LOOKUP_VOCABS.
# ----------------------------

def _activation_layer(x, act_name: str):
    if act_name == "gelu":
        return layers.Activation("gelu")(x)
    return layers.Activation(tf.keras.activations.silu)(x)  # default silu


def build_model_from_hp(hp):
    """
    KerasTuner model builder.
    Predicts log1p(price). Train/validate in log space; evaluate in dollars later.
    Uses a bounded tanh output head to prevent expm1 overflow.

    Expects these to exist (from Step 3):
      - NUM_NORM_WEIGHTS
      - LOOKUP_VOCABS
      - vocab_sizes (or recompute from LOOKUP_VOCABS)
      - embed_dims
    """

    # ----------------------------
    # Hyperparameters
    # ----------------------------
    act = hp.Choice("activation", ["silu", "gelu"], default=KERAS_TUNE["fallback_activation"])

    lr = hp.Float(
        "learning_rate", min_value=1e-4, max_value=3e-3, sampling="log",
        default=KERAS_TUNE["fallback_learning_rate"]
    )

    use_res = hp.Boolean("use_residuals", default=KERAS_TUNE["fallback_use_residuals"])

    l2 = hp.Choice("l2", values=[0.0, 1e-7, 1e-6, 1e-5], default=1e-6)
    emb_l2 = hp.Choice("embedding_l2", values=[0.0, 1e-7, 1e-6, 1e-5], default=1e-6)

    d1 = hp.Int("dense1", min_value=256, max_value=1024, step=256, default=512)
    d2 = hp.Int("dense2", min_value=128, max_value=512, step=128, default=256)
    d3 = hp.Int("dense3", min_value=64,  max_value=256, step=64,  default=128)

    drop1 = hp.Float("drop1", min_value=0.1, max_value=0.4, step=0.1, default=0.3)
    drop2 = hp.Float("drop2", min_value=0.1, max_value=0.4, step=0.1, default=0.25)
    drop3 = hp.Float("drop3", min_value=0.1, max_value=0.3, step=0.1, default=0.2)

    loss_name = hp.Choice("loss", ["mse", "mae", "huber"], default=KERAS_TUNE["fallback_loss"])
    huber_delta = hp.Float(
        "huber_delta", min_value=0.5, max_value=1.5, step=0.2,
        default=KERAS_TUNE["fallback_huber_delta"]
    )

    clipnorm = hp.Choice("clipnorm", values=[0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0], default=1.0)

    # --------------------------------------------------
    # SMOKE MODE SAFETY OVERRIDES
    # (do NOT change HP search space — override values)
    # --------------------------------------------------
    if TRAIN_MODE == "smoke":
        # Disable regularization pressure
        drop1 = min(drop1, 0.2)
        drop2 = min(drop2, 0.2)
        drop3 = min(drop3, 0.2)
        l2 = 0.0
        emb_l2 = 0.0

        # Make loss less median-seeking
        if loss_name == "huber":
            huber_delta = max(huber_delta, 1.0)

        # Encourage signal propagation
        use_res = True

    # ----------------------------
    # Output bounds for log1p(price)
    # ----------------------------
    # LOG_CENTER = 10.5
    # LOG_SCALE  = 6.0

    # log1p($500) ≈ 6.2,  log1p($500k) ≈ 13.1
    LOG_CENTER = 9.65   # midpoint of (6.2, 13.1)
    LOG_SCALE  = 3.45   # half-range of (6.2, 13.1)

    # ----------------------------
    # Inputs
    # ----------------------------
    numeric_in = keras.Input(shape=(len(num_cols_nn),), name="numeric", dtype=tf.float32)
    cat_inputs_local = {c: keras.Input(shape=(), name=c, dtype=tf.string) for c in cat_cols_nn}

    # ----------------------------
    # Fresh preprocessing layers per trial
    # ----------------------------
    # Fresh preprocessing layers per trial
    num_norm_local = layers.Normalization(name="num_norm")

    # Build before set_weights (otherwise expects 0 weights)
    num_norm_local.build((None, len(num_cols_nn)))

    # Now it can accept the adapted weights
    num_norm_local.set_weights(NUM_NORM_WEIGHTS)

    lookups_local = {}
    vocab_sizes_local = {}
    for c in cat_cols_nn:
        lk = layers.StringLookup(
            output_mode="int",
            mask_token=None,
            num_oov_indices=1,
            name=f"{c}_lookup"
        )
        # Use frozen vocab so every trial sees identical mapping
        lk.set_vocabulary(LOOKUP_VOCABS[c])
        lookups_local[c] = lk
        vocab_sizes_local[c] = int(lk.vocabulary_size())

    # ----------------------------
    # Numeric path
    # ----------------------------
    x_num = num_norm_local(numeric_in)

    # ----------------------------
    # Categorical embedding paths
    # ----------------------------
    emb_list = []
    emb_reg = keras.regularizers.l2(emb_l2) if (emb_l2 and emb_l2 > 0) else None

    # for c in cat_cols_nn:
    #     xi = lookups_local[c](cat_inputs_local[c])
    #     xi = layers.Embedding(
    #         input_dim=vocab_sizes_local[c],
    #         output_dim=embed_dims[c],
    #         embeddings_regularizer=emb_reg,
    #         name=f"{c}_emb"
    #     )(xi)
    #     xi = layers.Flatten()(xi)
    #     emb_list.append(xi)

    for c in cat_cols_nn:
        xi = lookups_local[c](cat_inputs_local[c])  # (batch,)
        xi = layers.Embedding(
            input_dim=vocab_sizes_local[c],
            output_dim=embed_dims[c],
            embeddings_regularizer=emb_reg,
            name=f"{c}_emb"
        )(xi)  # (batch, emb_dim)
        emb_list.append(xi)

    x = layers.Concatenate(name="concat")([x_num] + emb_list)

    reg = keras.regularizers.l2(l2) if (l2 and l2 > 0) else None

    # ----------------------------
    # Dense blocks
    # ----------------------------
    # Block 1 (d1)
    y = layers.Dense(d1, kernel_initializer="he_normal", kernel_regularizer=reg)(x)
    y = layers.BatchNormalization(momentum=0.9)(y)
    y = _activation_layer(y, act)
    y = layers.Dropout(drop1)(y)
    x = y

    # Block 2 (d2)
    y = layers.Dense(d2, kernel_initializer="he_normal", kernel_regularizer=reg)(x)
    y = layers.BatchNormalization(momentum=0.9)(y)
    y = _activation_layer(y, act)
    y = layers.Dropout(drop2)(y)
    if use_res:
        skip = x
        if int(skip.shape[-1]) != d2:
            skip = layers.Dense(d2, kernel_regularizer=reg)(skip)
        x = layers.Add()([y, skip])
    else:
        x = y

    # Block 3 (d3)
    y = layers.Dense(d3, kernel_initializer="he_normal", kernel_regularizer=reg)(x)
    y = layers.BatchNormalization(momentum=0.9)(y)
    y = _activation_layer(y, act)
    y = layers.Dropout(drop3)(y)
    if use_res:
        skip = x
        if int(skip.shape[-1]) != d3:
            skip = layers.Dense(d3, kernel_regularizer=reg)(skip)
        x = layers.Add()([y, skip])
    else:
        x = y

    # ----------------------------
    # Bounded output head (log1p)
    # ----------------------------
    z = layers.Dense(1, name="z_logit")(x)
    out = layers.Lambda(lambda t: LOG_CENTER + LOG_SCALE * tf.tanh(t), name="pred_log1p")(z)

    # REPLACE with this:
    # Output layer — NO regularizer, NO activity regularizer
    # out = layers.Dense(1, name="pred_log1p", 
    #                 kernel_initializer="glorot_uniform",
    #                 bias_initializer=tf.keras.initializers.Constant(LOG1P_MEAN),
    #                 kernel_regularizer=None,
    #                 kernel_constraint=MinMaxNorm(min_value=0.01, max_value=10.0, rate=1.0),
    #                 activation="softplus",)(x)


    # Raw linear output
    # z = layers.Dense(
    #     1,
    #     name="z_out",
    #     kernel_initializer="glorot_uniform",
    #     bias_initializer=tf.keras.initializers.Constant(LOG1P_MEAN),
    #     kernel_regularizer=None,
    #     kernel_constraint=tf.keras.constraints.MinMaxNorm(0.01, 10.0),
    # )(x)

    # # Softplus ensures positivity, clip ensures no astronomical outliers
    # # log1p(price) range: 0 (=$0) to 15 (≈$3.3M, well above any real car)
    # out = layers.Lambda(
    #     lambda t: tf.clip_by_value(tf.math.softplus(t), 0.0, 15.0),
    #     name="pred_log1p"
    # )(z)

    # ----------------------------
    # Output head (log1p) — linear
    # ----------------------------
    # out = layers.Dense(
    #     1,
    #     activation=None,            # linear output
    #     kernel_regularizer=reg,     # ✅ use the already-built regularizer (or None)
    #     name="price_log"
    # )(x)

    model_local = keras.Model(
        inputs={"numeric": numeric_in, **cat_inputs_local},
        outputs=out,
        name="tabular_nn_embeddings_tuned"
    )

    # Loss
    if loss_name == "huber":
        loss_fn = keras.losses.Huber(delta=huber_delta)
    else:
        loss_fn = loss_name

    model_local.compile(
        optimizer=keras.optimizers.Adam(learning_rate=lr, clipnorm=clipnorm),
        loss=loss_fn,
        metrics=[keras.metrics.RootMeanSquaredError(name="rmse_log")]
    )

    return model_local
```

#### Keras Model Training 
```{python}
# ----------------------------
# Step 6: Train or Load + Tuning (Hyperband -> Bayesian)
# ----------------------------
r'''
history = None
KERAS_RUN_DIR = None
model = None

SMOKE = (TRAIN_MODE == "smoke")

# consistent callbacks (weights-only checkpoints)
def make_callbacks(best_weights_path: str, train_mode: str):
    if train_mode == "smoke":
        ES_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["es_patience_smoke"]
        RL_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_patience_smoke"]
    else:
        ES_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["es_patience_full"]
        RL_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_patience_full"]

    return [
        keras.callbacks.EarlyStopping(
            monitor="val_loss",
            patience=ES_PAT,
            restore_best_weights=True
        ),
        keras.callbacks.ReduceLROnPlateau(
            monitor="val_loss",
            factor=KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_factor"],
            patience=RL_PAT,
            min_lr=KERAS_TUNE["min_lr"]
        ),
        keras.callbacks.ModelCheckpoint(
            filepath=best_weights_path,
            monitor="val_loss",
            save_best_only=True,
            save_weights_only=True
        ),
    ]

# -------------------------------------------------------
# TUNER callbacks (NO ModelCheckpoint)
# -------------------------------------------------------
def make_tuner_callbacks(train_mode: str):
    if train_mode == "smoke":
        ES_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["es_patience_smoke"]
        RL_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_patience_smoke"]
    else:
        ES_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["es_patience_full"]
        RL_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_patience_full"]

    return [
        keras.callbacks.EarlyStopping(
            monitor="val_loss",
            patience=ES_PAT,
            restore_best_weights=True
        ),
        keras.callbacks.ReduceLROnPlateau(
            monitor="val_loss",
            factor=KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_factor"],
            patience=RL_PAT,
            min_lr=KERAS_TUNE["min_lr"]
        ),
    ]

# write run info (kept)
def write_run_info(run_dir, extra: dict):
    info = {
        "train_mode": TRAIN_MODE,
        "timestamp": datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S"),
        "num_cols": list(num_cols_nn),
        "cat_cols": list(cat_cols_nn),
        "train_medians": {k: float(v) for k, v in train_medians.to_dict().items()},
        **extra
    }
    atomic_write_json(run_dir / "run_info.json", info)

# Minimal dataset wrapper (kept)
AUTOTUNE = tf.data.AUTOTUNE

def make_ds(inputs, y, batch_size: int, training: bool):
    n = int(len(y))
    if n == 0:
        raise ValueError("Dataset has 0 rows. Check your splits / filtering.")

    bs = int(min(batch_size, n))  # Never allow batch_size > n
    ds = tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices((inputs, y))

    if training:
        ds = ds.shuffle(min(n, 50_000), seed=SEED, reshuffle_each_iteration=True)

        # Only drop remainder if we'd still have at least 1 batch
        drop = (n >= bs) and (n // bs) > 0
        ds = ds.batch(bs, drop_remainder=drop)
    else:
        ds = ds.batch(bs, drop_remainder=False)

    return ds.prefetch(AUTOTUNE)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 1) SKIP mode: load latest if available
# -------------------------------------------------------
if TRAIN_MODE == "skip":
    if latest_path.exists():
        print("✅ TRAIN_MODE='skip' — loading latest Keras model:\n ", latest_path)
        model = keras.models.load_model(latest_path, compile=False)
    else:
        print("⏭️ TRAIN_MODE='skip' — no latest Keras model found, skipping.")
        model = None

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 2) FULL mode: load latest unless retraining
# -------------------------------------------------------
elif (TRAIN_MODE == "full") and latest_path.exists() and (not RETRAIN_KERAS):
    print("✅ Loading existing Keras latest model (RETRAIN_KERAS=False, TRAIN_MODE='full'):\n ", latest_path)
    model = keras.models.load_model(latest_path, compile=False)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Otherwise TRAIN/TUNE
# -------------------------------------------------------
else:
    if not WILL_TRAIN_KERAS:
        raise RuntimeError("WILL_TRAIN_KERAS=False but training branch entered. Check logic.")

    run_ts = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    KERAS_RUN_DIR = KERAS_RUNS_DIR / f"run_{run_ts}_{TRAIN_MODE}"
    KERAS_RUN_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    versioned_path = KERAS_RUN_DIR / "keras_model.keras"

    # ----------------------------
    # Tuner directories (SEPARATE)
    # ----------------------------
    # hb_dir = KERAS_RUN_DIR / "tuning" / "hyperband"
    # bo_dir = KERAS_RUN_DIR / "tuning" / "bayes"
    # hb_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    # bo_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # ----------------------------
    # Tuner directories (temp, local, disposable)
    # Final artifacts still saved under KERAS_RUN_DIR
    # ----------------------------
    tuning_root = Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / "used_car_kt" / f"run_{run_ts}_{TRAIN_MODE}"
    hb_dir = tuning_root / "hyperband"
    bo_dir = tuning_root / "bayes"

    # Start fresh every run
    if tuning_root.exists():
        shutil.rmtree(tuning_root, ignore_errors=True)

    hb_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    bo_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    HB_PROJECT = f"hb_{run_ts}"
    BO_PROJECT = f"bo_{run_ts}"

    print("tuning_root (TEMP) =", tuning_root)
    print("hb_dir =", hb_dir, "| HB_PROJECT =", HB_PROJECT)
    print("bo_dir =", bo_dir, "| BO_PROJECT =", BO_PROJECT)

    # ----------------------------
    # Budgets per mode
    # ----------------------------
    if SMOKE:
        EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["epochs_smoke"]
        HB_MAX_EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["hb_max_epochs_smoke"]
        BAYES_EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_epochs_smoke"]
        BAYES_TRIALS = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_max_trials_smoke"]
        BATCH = KERAS_TUNE["batch_smoke"]
        HB_EXEC = KERAS_TUNE["hb_executions_per_trial"]
        BAYES_EXEC = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_executions_per_trial"]
    else:
        EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["epochs_full"]
        HB_MAX_EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["hb_max_epochs_full"]
        BAYES_EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_epochs_full"]
        BAYES_TRIALS = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_max_trials_full"]
        BATCH = KERAS_TUNE["batch_full"]
        HB_EXEC = KERAS_TUNE["hb_executions_per_trial"]
        BAYES_EXEC = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_executions_per_trial"]

    # ----------------------------
    # Optional SMOKE subsampling (kept)
    # ----------------------------
    # if SMOKE and KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_subsample", False):
    #     rs = np.random.RandomState(SEED)

    #     ntr = min(int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_train_n", 20000)), len(y_train_nn))
    #     nva = min(int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_val_n", 5000)), len(y_val_nn))

    #     idx_tr = rs.choice(len(y_train_nn), size=ntr, replace=False)
    #     idx_va = rs.choice(len(y_val_nn),   size=nva, replace=False)

    #     train_inputs_run = {k: v[idx_tr] for k, v in train_inputs.items()}
    #     y_train_run = y_train_nn[idx_tr]

    #     val_inputs_run = {k: v[idx_va] for k, v in val_inputs.items()}
    #     y_val_run = y_val_nn[idx_va]

    #     print(
    #         f"🧪 Smoke subsample enabled: train={ntr:,} val={nva:,} | "
    #         f"batch={BATCH} | hb_epochs={HB_MAX_EPOCHS} | bayes_epochs={BAYES_EPOCHS} | bayes_trials={BAYES_TRIALS}"
    #     )
    # else:
    #     train_inputs_run, y_train_run = train_inputs, y_train_nn
    #     val_inputs_run, y_val_run = val_inputs, y_val_nn

    # ----------------------------
    # Optional SMOKE subsampling (SAFE: always convert to TF right after)
    # ----------------------------
    if SMOKE and KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_subsample", False):
        rs = np.random.RandomState(SEED)

        ntr = min(int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_train_n", 20000)), len(y_train_nn))
        nva = min(int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_val_n", 5000)), len(y_val_nn))

        idx_tr = rs.choice(len(y_train_nn), size=ntr, replace=False)
        idx_va = rs.choice(len(y_val_nn),   size=nva, replace=False)

        # NOTE: train_inputs/val_inputs are numpy-friendly dicts from to_input_dict()
        train_inputs_run_np = {k: v[idx_tr] for k, v in train_inputs.items()}
        y_train_run = y_train_nn[idx_tr]

        val_inputs_run_np = {k: v[idx_va] for k, v in val_inputs.items()}
        y_val_run = y_val_nn[idx_va]

        print(
            f"🧪 Smoke subsample enabled: train={ntr:,} val={nva:,} | "
            f"batch={BATCH} | hb_epochs={HB_MAX_EPOCHS} | bayes_epochs={BAYES_EPOCHS} | bayes_trials={BAYES_TRIALS}"
        )
    else:
        train_inputs_run_np, y_train_run = train_inputs, y_train_nn
        val_inputs_run_np,   y_val_run   = val_inputs,   y_val_nn

    # ✅ Convert to TF *once* and use ONLY these for make_ds / fit / predict
    train_inputs_run_tf = to_tf_inputs(train_inputs_run_np)
    val_inputs_run_tf   = to_tf_inputs(val_inputs_run_np)

    # 🔒 Optional safety: prevent accidental use of numpy dicts later (recommended)
    train_inputs_run_np = None
    val_inputs_run_np   = None

    # Create datasets once and reuse for tuner + final fit
    # train_ds = make_ds(train_inputs_run, y_train_run, BATCH, training=True)
    # val_ds   = make_ds(val_inputs_run,   y_val_run,   BATCH, training=False)

    train_ds = make_ds(train_inputs_run_tf, y_train_run, BATCH, training=True)
    val_ds   = make_ds(val_inputs_run_tf,   y_val_run,   BATCH, training=False)

    # Objective: minimize validation loss in log space
    objective = kt.Objective("val_loss", direction="min")

    print("num feature count =", len(num_cols_nn))
    print("cat feature count =", len(cat_cols_nn))
    print("train numeric shape =", train_inputs_run_tf["numeric"].shape)
    print("one cat example shape =", train_inputs_run_tf[cat_cols_nn[0]].shape if cat_cols_nn else None)
    print("y_train shape =", y_train_run.shape)

    # ----------------------------
    # TUNING STAGE 1 (coarse): Hyperband
    # ----------------------------
    print("🧭 TUNING STAGE 1 (coarse): Hyperband")
    gc.collect()

    hb_callbacks = make_tuner_callbacks(TRAIN_MODE)

    hb = kt.Hyperband(
        hypermodel=build_model_from_hp,
        objective=objective,
        max_epochs=HB_MAX_EPOCHS,
        factor=KERAS_TUNE["hb_factor"],
        executions_per_trial=HB_EXEC,
        directory=str(hb_dir),
        project_name=HB_PROJECT,
        overwrite=True,
    )

    hb.search(
        train_ds,
        validation_data=val_ds,
        epochs=HB_MAX_EPOCHS,
        callbacks=hb_callbacks,
        verbose=0,
    )

    best_hp_hb = hb.get_best_hyperparameters(1)[0]
    print("✅ Best HP from Hyperband:", best_hp_hb.values)

    # ----------------------------
    # TUNING STAGE 2 (fine): BayesianOptimization
    # ----------------------------
    print("🎯 TUNING STAGE 2 (fine): BayesianOptimization")
    tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
    gc.collect()

    bo_callbacks = make_tuner_callbacks(TRAIN_MODE)

    bo = kt.BayesianOptimization(
        hypermodel=build_model_from_hp,
        objective=objective,
        max_trials=BAYES_TRIALS,
        executions_per_trial=BAYES_EXEC,
        directory=str(bo_dir),
        project_name=BO_PROJECT,
        overwrite=True,
    )

    bo.search(
        train_ds,
        validation_data=val_ds,
        epochs=BAYES_EPOCHS,
        callbacks=bo_callbacks,
        verbose=0,
    )

    best_hp = bo.get_best_hyperparameters(1)[0]
    print("🏆 Best HP from BayesianOptimization:", best_hp.values)

    # ----------------------------
    # FINAL FIT using best HP (FROM SCRATCH) — stable path
    # ----------------------------
    print("🔁 Final training with best hyperparameters (from scratch)")
    tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
    gc.collect()

    model = build_model_from_hp(best_hp)

    # ----------------------------
    # DEBUG
    # ----------------------------
    # ✅ In SMOKE: rely on EarlyStopping(restore_best_weights=True) ONLY.
    # Do NOT use ModelCheckpoint weights in smoke (they've been degenerating).
    if TRAIN_MODE == "smoke":
        final_callbacks = make_tuner_callbacks(TRAIN_MODE)  # ES + RLRP only
        final_best_weights_path = None
    else:
        final_best_weights_path = str(KERAS_RUN_DIR / "best_final.weights.h5")
        final_callbacks = make_callbacks(final_best_weights_path, TRAIN_MODE)

    # ----------------------------
    # DEBUG
    # ----------------------------
# ADD BACK LATER
    # final_best_weights_path = str(KERAS_RUN_DIR / "best_final.weights.h5")
    # final_callbacks = make_callbacks(final_best_weights_path, TRAIN_MODE)

    history = model.fit(
        train_ds,
        validation_data=val_ds,
        epochs=EPOCHS,
        verbose=0,
        callbacks=final_callbacks
    )

    # ==========================================================
    # DEBUG 
    # STEP 3: Immediate post-training prediction variance check
    # Place this IMMEDIATELY after:
    #   history = model.fit(...)
    # and BEFORE any load_weights(), model.save(), clear_session(), or reloads.
    # ==========================================================
    print("\n--- STEP 3: immediate post-fit prediction variance check ---")

    dbg_inputs = {}

    # Use the TF-safe dict you already created for datasets:
    #   val_inputs_run_tf
    for k, v in val_inputs_run_tf.items():
        if k == "numeric":
            dbg_inputs[k] = v[:2048, :]
        else:
            # v is already tf.string tensor; keep shape consistent
            dbg_inputs[k] = v[:2048]

    pred_now = model.predict(dbg_inputs, batch_size=256, verbose=0).ravel()
    pred_now = np.asarray(pred_now, dtype="float64")

    print(
        "pred_now: std=",
        float(np.std(pred_now)),
        "min/med/max=",
        float(np.min(pred_now)),
        float(np.median(pred_now)),
        float(np.max(pred_now)),
    )

    true_now = y_val_run[:2048].ravel().astype("float64")
    print(
        "true_now: std=",
        float(np.std(true_now)),
        "min/med/max=",
        float(np.min(true_now)),
        float(np.median(true_now)),
        float(np.max(true_now)),
    )

    ratio = float(np.std(pred_now) / (np.std(true_now) + 1e-12))
    print("std(pred_now) / std(true_now) =", ratio)

    # ✅ IMPORTANT: Remove/disable ANY manual load_weights in SMOKE
    if (TRAIN_MODE != "smoke") and final_best_weights_path and os.path.exists(final_best_weights_path):
        model.load_weights(final_best_weights_path)
        print("✅ Loaded best FINAL weights:", final_best_weights_path)
    else:
        print("ℹ️ Using EarlyStopping-restored weights (no manual load_weights).")

    # ==========================================================
    # END OF DEBUG
    # ==========================================================
# Add BACK LATER
    # Load best FINAL weights explicitly (weights-only checkpoint)

    # if os.path.exists(final_best_weights_path):
    #     model.load_weights(final_best_weights_path)
    #     print("✅ Loaded best FINAL weights:", final_best_weights_path)
    # else:
    #     print("⚠️ Best weights file not found:", final_best_weights_path)

   
    _ = model(
    {
        "numeric": val_inputs_run_tf["numeric"][:2, :],
        **{c: val_inputs_run_tf[c][:2] for c in cat_cols_nn}
    },
    training=False
)

    # Save FULL model artifact (versioned)
    model.save(versioned_path, include_optimizer=False)
    print("💾 Saved versioned FULL model:", versioned_path)

    # Update "latest" only for full mode
    if TRAIN_MODE == "full":
        model.save(latest_path, include_optimizer=False)
        print("✅ Updated latest FULL model:", latest_path)
    else:
        print("🚫 Smoke run: NOT updating latest pointer.")

    # Persist tuning results + chosen hp (kept)
    write_run_info(KERAS_RUN_DIR, extra={
        "tuning": {
            "strategy": "hyperband_then_bayes",
            "hyperband_best": dict(best_hp_hb.values),
            "bayes_best": dict(best_hp.values),
            "hb_max_epochs": int(HB_MAX_EPOCHS),
            "bayes_epochs": int(BAYES_EPOCHS),
            "bayes_max_trials": int(BAYES_TRIALS),
            "hyperband_dir": str(hb_dir),
            "bayes_dir": str(bo_dir),
            "smoke_subsample": bool(SMOKE and KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_subsample", False)),
            "smoke_train_n": int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_train_n", 0)) if SMOKE else None,
            "smoke_val_n": int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_val_n", 0)) if SMOKE else None,
            "batch": int(BATCH),
            "final_epochs_cap": int(EPOCHS),
        }
    })

    atomic_write_json(KERAS_RUN_DIR / "best_hp.json", dict(best_hp.values))
    print("🧾 Wrote best_hp.json:", KERAS_RUN_DIR / "best_hp.json")
'''
```

#### Training V2
```{python}
# ----------------------------
# Step 6: Train or Load + Tuning (Hyperband -> Bayesian)
# ----------------------------

history = None
KERAS_RUN_DIR = None
model = None

SMOKE = (TRAIN_MODE == "smoke")

# ----------------------------
# Callbacks
# ----------------------------
def make_callbacks(best_weights_path: str, train_mode: str):
    """Final fit callbacks — always uses ModelCheckpoint to own best-weight tracking."""
    ES_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["es_patience_smoke"] if train_mode == "smoke" else KERAS_TUNE["es_patience_full"]
    RL_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_patience_smoke"] if train_mode == "smoke" else KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_patience_full"]

    return [
        keras.callbacks.EarlyStopping(
            monitor="val_loss",
            patience=ES_PAT,
            restore_best_weights=False,  # ModelCheckpoint owns this; avoids in-memory restore race
        ),
        keras.callbacks.ReduceLROnPlateau(
            monitor="val_loss",
            factor=KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_factor"],
            patience=RL_PAT,
            min_lr=KERAS_TUNE["min_lr"],
        ),
        keras.callbacks.ModelCheckpoint(
            filepath=best_weights_path,
            monitor="val_loss",
            save_best_only=True,
            save_weights_only=True,
        ),
    ]


def make_tuner_callbacks(train_mode: str):
    """Tuner-trial callbacks — no ModelCheckpoint (KerasTuner manages its own state)."""
    ES_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["es_patience_smoke"] if train_mode == "smoke" else KERAS_TUNE["es_patience_full"]
    RL_PAT = KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_patience_smoke"] if train_mode == "smoke" else KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_patience_full"]

    return [
        keras.callbacks.EarlyStopping(
            monitor="val_loss",
            patience=ES_PAT,
            restore_best_weights=True,  # fine for tuner trials (in-memory only, not persisted)
        ),
        keras.callbacks.ReduceLROnPlateau(
            monitor="val_loss",
            factor=KERAS_TUNE["rlrop_factor"],
            patience=RL_PAT,
            min_lr=KERAS_TUNE["min_lr"],
        ),
    ]


# ----------------------------
# Helpers
# ----------------------------
def write_run_info(run_dir, extra: dict):
    info = {
        "train_mode": TRAIN_MODE,
        "timestamp": datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S"),
        "num_cols": list(num_cols_nn),
        "cat_cols": list(cat_cols_nn),
        "train_medians": {k: float(v) for k, v in train_medians.to_dict().items()},
        **extra,
    }
    atomic_write_json(run_dir / "run_info.json", info)


AUTOTUNE = tf.data.AUTOTUNE


def make_ds(inputs, y, batch_size: int, training: bool):
    n = int(len(y))
    if n == 0:
        raise ValueError("Dataset has 0 rows. Check your splits / filtering.")

    bs = int(min(batch_size, n))
    ds = tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices((inputs, y))

    if training:
        ds = ds.shuffle(min(n, 50_000), seed=SEED, reshuffle_each_iteration=True)
        # ✅ Only drop remainder if at least 2 full batches exist
        # Prevents empty final batch causing clipnorm shape mismatch in Adam
        drop = (n // bs) >= 2
        ds = ds.batch(bs, drop_remainder=drop)
    else:
        ds = ds.batch(bs, drop_remainder=False)

    return ds.prefetch(AUTOTUNE)


# -------------------------------------------------------
# 1) SKIP mode
# -------------------------------------------------------
if TRAIN_MODE == "skip":
    if latest_path.exists():
        print("✅ TRAIN_MODE='skip' — loading latest Keras model:\n ", latest_path)
        model = keras.models.load_model(latest_path, compile=False)
    else:
        print("⏭️ TRAIN_MODE='skip' — no latest Keras model found, skipping.")
        model = None

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 2) FULL mode: load latest unless retraining
# -------------------------------------------------------
elif (TRAIN_MODE == "full") and latest_path.exists() and (not RETRAIN_KERAS):
    print("✅ Loading existing Keras latest model (RETRAIN_KERAS=False, TRAIN_MODE='full'):\n ", latest_path)
    model = keras.models.load_model(latest_path, compile=False)

# -------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Train / tune
# -------------------------------------------------------
else:
    if not WILL_TRAIN_KERAS:
        raise RuntimeError("WILL_TRAIN_KERAS=False but training branch entered. Check logic.")

    run_ts = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    KERAS_RUN_DIR = KERAS_RUNS_DIR / f"run_{run_ts}_{TRAIN_MODE}"
    KERAS_RUN_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    versioned_path = KERAS_RUN_DIR / "keras_model.keras"

    # Tuner dirs in temp (disposable)
    tuning_root = Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / "used_car_kt" / f"run_{run_ts}_{TRAIN_MODE}"
    hb_dir = tuning_root / "hyperband"
    bo_dir = tuning_root / "bayes"
    if tuning_root.exists():
        shutil.rmtree(tuning_root, ignore_errors=True)
    hb_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    bo_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    HB_PROJECT = f"hb_{run_ts}"
    BO_PROJECT = f"bo_{run_ts}"

    print("tuning_root (TEMP) =", tuning_root)
    print("hb_dir =", hb_dir, "| HB_PROJECT =", HB_PROJECT)
    print("bo_dir =", bo_dir, "| BO_PROJECT =", BO_PROJECT)

    # Budgets per mode
    if SMOKE:
        EPOCHS       = KERAS_TUNE["epochs_smoke"]
        HB_MAX_EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["hb_max_epochs_smoke"]
        BAYES_EPOCHS  = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_epochs_smoke"]
        BAYES_TRIALS  = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_max_trials_smoke"]
        BATCH         = KERAS_TUNE["batch_smoke"]
    else:
        EPOCHS        = KERAS_TUNE["epochs_full"]
        HB_MAX_EPOCHS = KERAS_TUNE["hb_max_epochs_full"]
        BAYES_EPOCHS  = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_epochs_full"]
        BAYES_TRIALS  = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_max_trials_full"]
        BATCH         = KERAS_TUNE["batch_full"]

    HB_EXEC   = KERAS_TUNE["hb_executions_per_trial"]
    BAYES_EXEC = KERAS_TUNE["bayes_executions_per_trial"]

    # Optional smoke subsampling
    if SMOKE and KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_subsample", False):
        rs  = np.random.RandomState(SEED)
        ntr = min(int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_train_n", 20000)), len(y_train_nn))
        nva = min(int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_val_n",   5000)),  len(y_val_nn))

        idx_tr = rs.choice(len(y_train_nn), size=ntr, replace=False)
        idx_va = rs.choice(len(y_val_nn),   size=nva, replace=False)

        train_inputs_run_np = {k: v[idx_tr] for k, v in train_inputs.items()}
        y_train_run         = y_train_nn[idx_tr]
        val_inputs_run_np   = {k: v[idx_va] for k, v in val_inputs.items()}
        y_val_run           = y_val_nn[idx_va]

        print(
            f"🧪 Smoke subsample: train={ntr:,} val={nva:,} | "
            f"batch={BATCH} | hb_epochs={HB_MAX_EPOCHS} | "
            f"bayes_epochs={BAYES_EPOCHS} | bayes_trials={BAYES_TRIALS}"
        )
    else:
        train_inputs_run_np, y_train_run = train_inputs, y_train_nn
        val_inputs_run_np,   y_val_run   = val_inputs,   y_val_nn

    # Convert to TF once; nullify numpy refs
    train_inputs_run_tf = to_tf_inputs(train_inputs_run_np)
    val_inputs_run_tf   = to_tf_inputs(val_inputs_run_np)
    train_inputs_run_np = None
    val_inputs_run_np   = None

    train_ds = make_ds(train_inputs_run_tf, y_train_run, BATCH, training=True)
    val_ds   = make_ds(val_inputs_run_tf,   y_val_run,   BATCH, training=False)

    objective = kt.Objective("val_loss", direction="min")

    print("num feature count =", len(num_cols_nn))
    print("cat feature count =", len(cat_cols_nn))
    print("train numeric shape =", train_inputs_run_tf["numeric"].shape)
    print("one cat example shape =", train_inputs_run_tf[cat_cols_nn[0]].shape if cat_cols_nn else None)
    print("y_train shape =", y_train_run.shape)

    # ----------------------------
    # TUNING STAGE 1: Hyperband
    # ----------------------------
    print("🧭 TUNING STAGE 1 (coarse): Hyperband")
    gc.collect()

    hb = kt.Hyperband(
        hypermodel=build_model_from_hp,
        objective=objective,
        max_epochs=HB_MAX_EPOCHS,
        factor=KERAS_TUNE["hb_factor"],
        executions_per_trial=HB_EXEC,
        directory=str(hb_dir),
        project_name=HB_PROJECT,
        overwrite=True,
    )
    hb.search(
        train_ds,
        validation_data=val_ds,
        epochs=HB_MAX_EPOCHS,
        callbacks=make_tuner_callbacks(TRAIN_MODE),
        verbose=0,
    )
    best_hp_hb = hb.get_best_hyperparameters(1)[0]
    print("✅ Best HP from Hyperband:", best_hp_hb.values)

    # ----------------------------
    # TUNING STAGE 2: BayesianOptimization
    # ----------------------------
    print("🎯 TUNING STAGE 2 (fine): BayesianOptimization")
    tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
    gc.collect()

    bo = kt.BayesianOptimization(
        hypermodel=build_model_from_hp,
        objective=objective,
        max_trials=BAYES_TRIALS,
        executions_per_trial=BAYES_EXEC,
        directory=str(bo_dir),
        project_name=BO_PROJECT,
        overwrite=True,
    )
    bo.search(
        train_ds,
        validation_data=val_ds,
        epochs=BAYES_EPOCHS,
        callbacks=make_tuner_callbacks(TRAIN_MODE),
        verbose=0,
    )
    best_hp = bo.get_best_hyperparameters(1)[0]
    print("🏆 Best HP from BayesianOptimization:", best_hp.values)

    # ----------------------------
    # FINAL FIT from scratch with best HP
    # ----------------------------
    print("🔁 Final training with best hyperparameters (from scratch)")
    tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
    gc.collect()

    # ✅ Rebuild datasets AFTER clear_session() — the old tf.data graphs were invalidated
    train_ds_final = make_ds(train_inputs_run_tf, y_train_run, BATCH, training=True)
    val_ds_final   = make_ds(val_inputs_run_tf,   y_val_run,   BATCH, training=False)

    # ----------------------------
    # Sanity-clamp best_hp before final fit
    # Prevents tuner from handing us pathological configs
    # that work at tuner-budget epochs but collapse at full training length
    # ----------------------------
    def _safe_hp_values(hp_obj, train_mode: str) -> dict:
        """
        Return a cleaned copy of HP values with safety floors/ceilings applied.
        These constraints apply in ALL modes — they encode known-bad regions
        that the tuner can stumble into due to budget mismatch.
        """
        v = dict(hp_obj.values)

        v["output_l2"] = 0.0

        # huber_delta < 0.5 approaches MAE and causes median collapse
        if v.get("loss") == "huber":
            v["huber_delta"] = max(v.get("huber_delta", 1.0), 0.5)

        # High dropout + no residuals = dead signal, especially in deeper networks
        if not v.get("use_residuals", True):
            v["drop1"] = min(v.get("drop1", 0.3), 0.3)
            v["drop2"] = min(v.get("drop2", 0.3), 0.3)
            v["drop3"] = min(v.get("drop3", 0.2), 0.2)

        return v


    best_hp_values = _safe_hp_values(best_hp, TRAIN_MODE)
    print("🔒 Clamped HP values for final fit:", best_hp_values)

    # Rebuild a clean HyperParameters object from clamped values

    best_hp_clamped = kt.HyperParameters()
    for k, v in best_hp_values.items():
        if not k.startswith("tuner/"):   # skip tuner internal keys
            best_hp_clamped.Fixed(k, v)

    model = build_model_from_hp(best_hp_clamped)

    # model = build_model_from_hp(best_hp)

    final_best_weights_path = str(KERAS_RUN_DIR / "best_final.weights.h5")
    final_callbacks = make_callbacks(final_best_weights_path, TRAIN_MODE)

    history = model.fit(
        train_ds_final,
        validation_data=val_ds_final,
        epochs=EPOCHS,
        verbose=0,
        callbacks=final_callbacks,
    )

    # Explicitly load best weights from disk
    if os.path.exists(final_best_weights_path):
        model.load_weights(final_best_weights_path)
        print("✅ Loaded best weights from checkpoint:", final_best_weights_path)
    else:
        print("⚠️ No checkpoint found — using final-epoch weights")

    # 2. BatchNorm calibration — fixes running statistics
    print("🔧 Calibrating BatchNorm running statistics...")
    for layer in model.layers:
        if isinstance(layer, tf.keras.layers.BatchNormalization):
            layer.moving_mean.assign(tf.zeros_like(layer.moving_mean))
            layer.moving_variance.assign(tf.ones_like(layer.moving_variance))

    @tf.function
    def calibration_step(x_batch):
        model(x_batch, training=True)

    calib_ds = make_ds(train_inputs_run_tf, y_train_run, BATCH, training=False).take(100)
    for x_batch, _ in calib_ds:
        calibration_step(x_batch)
    print("✅ BatchNorm calibration complete")

    # 3. Warm-up forward pass — ensures graph is fully traced before saving
    _ = model(
        {
            "numeric": val_inputs_run_tf["numeric"][:2, :],
            **{c: val_inputs_run_tf[c][:2] for c in cat_cols_nn},
        },
        training=False,
    )

    model.save(versioned_path, include_optimizer=False)
    print("💾 Saved versioned model:", versioned_path)

    if TRAIN_MODE == "full":
        model.save(latest_path, include_optimizer=False)
        print("✅ Updated latest model:", latest_path)
    else:
        print("🚫 Smoke run: NOT updating latest pointer.")

    # Persist run metadata
    write_run_info(KERAS_RUN_DIR, extra={
        "tuning": {
            "strategy": "hyperband_then_bayes",
            "hyperband_best": dict(best_hp_hb.values),
            "bayes_best": dict(best_hp.values),
            "hb_max_epochs": int(HB_MAX_EPOCHS),
            "bayes_epochs": int(BAYES_EPOCHS),
            "bayes_max_trials": int(BAYES_TRIALS),
            "hyperband_dir": str(hb_dir),
            "bayes_dir": str(bo_dir),
            "smoke_subsample": bool(SMOKE and KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_subsample", False)),
            "smoke_train_n": int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_train_n", 0)) if SMOKE else None,
            "smoke_val_n": int(KERAS_TUNE.get("smoke_val_n", 0)) if SMOKE else None,
            "batch": int(BATCH),
            "final_epochs_cap": int(EPOCHS),
        }
    })

    atomic_write_json(KERAS_RUN_DIR / "best_hp.json", dict(best_hp.values))
    print("🧾 Wrote best_hp.json:", KERAS_RUN_DIR / "best_hp.json")
```

```{python}
print("pred_log min/med/max:", pred_log.min(), np.median(pred_log), pred_log.max())
print("pred $ max:", np.expm1(pred_log.max()))

# =====================================================================
# LAYER-BY-LAYER VARIANCE PROBE
# =====================================================================

# Use a small batch of test data with known diversity
probe_size = 512
probe_inputs = {
    "numeric": test_inputs_tf["numeric"][:probe_size],
    **{c: test_inputs_tf[c][:probe_size] for c in cat_cols_nn}
}

# Build intermediate models for each key layer
layer_names_to_probe = [l.name for l in model.layers]
print(f"Total layers: {len(layer_names_to_probe)}")
print()

# Probe every layer that has a meaningful output
for layer in model.layers:
    try:
        intermediate = tf.keras.Model(inputs=model.inputs, outputs=layer.output)
        out = intermediate(probe_inputs, training=False).numpy()
        flat = out.flatten()
        print(f"{layer.name:45s}  shape={str(out.shape):20s}  "
              f"std={flat.std():.6f}  min={flat.min():.4f}  max={flat.max():.4f}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{layer.name:45s}  SKIP ({e})")

# =====================================================================
# PREPROCESSING OUTPUT PROBE
# =====================================================================
# Check what the normalization layer actually outputs
norm_layer = model.get_layer("num_norm")  # adjust name if different
norm_model = tf.keras.Model(inputs=model.get_layer(index=0).input 
                             if False else model.inputs, 
                             outputs=norm_layer.output)
norm_out = norm_model(probe_inputs, training=False).numpy()
print(f"\nAfter normalization: shape={norm_out.shape}")
print(f"  std per feature (first 5): {norm_out.std(axis=0)[:5]}")
print(f"  overall std: {norm_out.std():.6f}")
print(f"  near-zero std features: {(norm_out.std(axis=0) < 0.01).sum()} / {norm_out.shape[1]}")

# ==========================================================
# DEBUG helper: force static shapes for model inputs
# (local to debug cell)
# ==========================================================
def _force_static_shapes_debug(inputs: dict) -> dict:
    """
    Match EXACT model input signatures:
      - numeric: (None, d_num) float32
      - categoricals: (None,) tf.string
    """
    d_num = len(num_cols_nn)
    cat_keys = list(cat_cols_nn)

    out = {}

    # numeric
    xnum = tf.convert_to_tensor(inputs["numeric"], dtype=tf.float32)
    if xnum.shape.rank != 2 or xnum.shape[-1] != d_num:
        xnum = tf.reshape(xnum, [-1, d_num])
    xnum.set_shape([None, d_num])
    out["numeric"] = xnum

    # categoricals
    for k in cat_keys:
        x = tf.convert_to_tensor(inputs[k], dtype=tf.string)

        # force rank-1 (None,)
        if x.shape.rank == 2 and x.shape[-1] == 1:
            x = tf.reshape(x, [-1])
        elif x.shape.rank != 1:
            x = tf.reshape(x, [-1])

        x.set_shape([None])
        out[k] = x

    return out

# print("|z| > 3:", float(np.mean(np.abs(z_vals) > 3.0))*100, "%")
# print("out (log1p) min/med/max:", float(np.min(out_vals)), float(np.median(out_vals)), float(np.max(out_vals)))
# print("out std:", float(np.std(out_vals)))
# print("="*70 + "\n")

# ==========================================================
# CONSISTENCY CHECK: three ways to get pred_log
# - direct model call (no adapters)
# - model.predict on dict (adapter)
# - model.predict on tf.data (adapter)
# Goal: they should have similar std/range
# ==========================================================
def _summ(name, arr):
    arr = np.asarray(arr).reshape(-1).astype("float64")
    print(
        f"{name}: n={len(arr)} "
        f"min/med/max={arr.min():.4f} / {np.median(arr):.4f} / {arr.max():.4f} "
        f"std={arr.std():.6f}"
    )

fixed = _force_static_shapes_debug(test_inputs_tf)

# Use a decent slice so it's not just a tiny batch artifact
NCHK = 8192
slice_inputs = {k: (v[:NCHK, :] if k == "numeric" else v[:NCHK]) for k, v in fixed.items()}

# (A) DIRECT call (no model.predict adapter)
pred_direct = model(slice_inputs, training=False).numpy().reshape(-1)
_summ("pred_direct", pred_direct)

# (B) model.predict on dict
pred_predict = model.predict(slice_inputs, batch_size=1024, verbose=0).reshape(-1)
_summ("pred_predict(dict)", pred_predict)

# (C) model.predict via tf.data
pred_ds = tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices(slice_inputs).batch(1024)
pred_predict_ds = model.predict(pred_ds, verbose=0).reshape(-1)
_summ("pred_predict(tf.data)", pred_predict_ds)

# Quick agreement checks
print("\nmax |direct - predict(dict)|:", float(np.max(np.abs(pred_direct - pred_predict))))
print("max |direct - predict(ds)|  :", float(np.max(np.abs(pred_direct - pred_predict_ds))))


# # ----------------------------------------------------------
```
