```{python}
# print("=" * 70 + "\n")

def _pct(x): 
    return 100.0 * float(x)

print("\n" + "="*70)
print("KERAS DEBUG SNAPSHOT")
print("="*70)

# ---- basic state ----
print("TRAIN_MODE:", TRAIN_MODE)
print("RETRAIN_KERAS:", RETRAIN_KERAS)
print("WILL_TRAIN_KERAS:", globals().get("WILL_TRAIN_KERAS", None))
print("KERAS_RUN_DIR:", globals().get("KERAS_RUN_DIR", None))
print("latest_path:", globals().get("latest_path", None))

# ---- y-shape sanity (CRITICAL) ----
print("\n--- y shape sanity ---")
print("y_train_nn shape:", y_train_nn.shape)
print("y_val_nn shape:", y_val_nn.shape)
print("y_test_nn shape:", y_test_nn.shape)

assert y_train_nn.ndim == 2 and y_train_nn.shape[1] == 1, "y_train_nn must be (N, 1)"
assert y_val_nn.ndim   == 2 and y_val_nn.shape[1]   == 1, "y_val_nn must be (N, 1)"
assert y_test_nn.ndim  == 2 and y_test_nn.shape[1]  == 1, "y_test_nn must be (N, 1)"

if ("model" not in globals()) or (model is None):
    print("❌ model is None (training/loading did not produce a model). Stop here.")
else:
    # ---- input sanity ----
    print("\n--- Input dict sanity ---")
    print("len(num_cols_nn):", len(num_cols_nn))
    print("len(cat_cols_nn):", len(cat_cols_nn))
    print("numeric matrix shape:", test_inputs_tf["numeric"].shape)
    if len(cat_cols_nn) > 0:
        c0 = cat_cols_nn[0]
        print("example cat:", c0, "dtype:", test_inputs_tf[c0].dtype, "shape:", test_inputs_tf[c0].shape)
        print("example cat sample:", test_inputs_tf[c0][:5])

    # ---- model input shapes ----
    print("\n--- Model inputs ---")
    for inp in model.inputs:
        print(" ", inp.name, inp.shape, inp.dtype)

    # ---- target distribution sanity ----
    print("\n--- y (log1p) sanity ---")
    print("y_test_nn min/median/max:",
          float(np.min(y_test_nn)), float(np.median(y_test_nn)), float(np.max(y_test_nn)))
    print("y_test_nn std:", float(np.std(y_test_nn)))

    # ---- prediction distribution sanity (log) ----
    # print("\n--- pred_log sanity ---")
    # pred_log = model.predict(test_inputs_tf, batch_size=KERAS_TUNE["pred_batch"], verbose=0).ravel()
    # pred_log = np.asarray(pred_log, dtype="float64")

    # print("pred_log min/median/max:",
    #       float(np.min(pred_log)), float(np.median(pred_log)), float(np.max(pred_log)))
    # print("pred_log std:", float(np.std(pred_log)))

    print("\n--- pred_log sanity (FULL TEST via tf.data) ---")

    # Build a predict-only dataset from the exact same test_inputs_tf dict
    # pred_bs = int(KERAS_TUNE.get("pred_batch", 1024))
    # test_pred_ds = tf.data.Dataset.from_tensor_slices(test_inputs_tf).batch(pred_bs)

    # pred_log = model.predict(test_pred_ds, verbose=0).reshape(-1).astype("float64")

    # true_log = y_test_nn.reshape(-1).astype("float64")

    # print("pred_log min/median/max:",
    #     float(np.min(pred_log)), float(np.median(pred_log)), float(np.max(pred_log)))
    # print("pred_log std:", float(np.std(pred_log)))

    # print("true_log std:", float(np.std(true_log)))
    # print("ratio:", float(np.std(pred_log) / (np.std(true_log) + 1e-12)))

    # --- pred_log sanity (FULL TEST, eager call; avoids predict() + tf.data tracing) ---

    # Force exact static shapes/ranks that match your model inputs
    fixed_test = _force_static_shapes_debug(test_inputs_tf)  # numeric:(None,d), cats:(None,)

    # Direct forward pass (no model.predict adapter)
    pred_log = model(fixed_test, training=False).numpy().reshape(-1).astype("float64")

    true_log = y_test_nn.reshape(-1).astype("float64")

    print("pred_log min/median/max:",
        float(np.min(pred_log)), float(np.median(pred_log)), float(np.max(pred_log)))
    print("pred_log std:", float(np.std(pred_log)))

    print("true_log std:", float(np.std(true_log)))
    print("ratio:", float(np.std(pred_log) / (np.std(true_log) + 1e-12)))

    log_mae = float(np.mean(np.abs(pred_log - true_log)))
    print("log-space MAE:", log_mae)

    # How constant is it?
    const_ratio = float(np.std(pred_log) / (np.std(y_test_nn) + 1e-12))
    print("std(pred_log) / std(true_log):", const_ratio,
          "  (<< 1.0 means near-constant predictions)")

    # ---- quick log-space MAE ----
    log_mae = float(np.mean(np.abs(pred_log - y_test_nn.astype("float64"))))
    print("log-space MAE:", log_mae)

    # ---- dollar-space quick sanity (no full metrics, just range) ----
    pred_dollars = np.expm1(np.clip(pred_log, -5, 20))  # clip to avoid overflow in debug
    true_dollars = np.expm1(y_test_nn.astype("float64"))
    print("\n--- $ range sanity ---")
    print("true $ min/median/max:",
          float(np.min(true_dollars)), float(np.median(true_dollars)), float(np.max(true_dollars)))
    print("pred $ min/median/max:",
          float(np.min(pred_dollars)), float(np.median(pred_dollars)), float(np.max(pred_dollars)))

    # ---- schema/order check against run_info.json if available ----
    print("\n--- Schema check vs run_info.json ---")
    run_dir = globals().get("KERAS_RUN_DIR", None)
    if run_dir is not None:
        run_info_path = Path(run_dir) / "run_info.json"
        if run_info_path.exists():
            info = json.loads(run_info_path.read_text())
            saved_num = info.get("num_cols", None)
            saved_cat = info.get("cat_cols", None)

            print("run_info.json found:", run_info_path)
            print("num cols exact match?", (saved_num == list(num_cols_nn)))
            print("cat cols exact match?", (saved_cat == list(cat_cols_nn)))

            if saved_num != list(num_cols_nn):
                # show first mismatch
                for i, (a, b) in enumerate(zip(saved_num, list(num_cols_nn))):
                    if a != b:
                        print("FIRST NUM MISMATCH @", i, "| saved:", a, "| current:", b)
                        break
                print("saved_num_len:", len(saved_num), "current_len:", len(num_cols_nn))

            if saved_cat != list(cat_cols_nn):
                for i, (a, b) in enumerate(zip(saved_cat, list(cat_cols_nn))):
                    if a != b:
                        print("FIRST CAT MISMATCH @", i, "| saved:", a, "| current:", b)
                        break
                print("saved_cat_len:", len(saved_cat), "current_len:", len(cat_cols_nn))
        else:
            print("No run_info.json in KERAS_RUN_DIR (maybe loaded model / older run).")
    else:
        print("KERAS_RUN_DIR is None (likely loaded latest or training skipped).")

print("="*70 + "\n")

print("history keys:", list(history.history.keys()))
print("first/last loss:", history.history["loss"][0], history.history["loss"][-1])
print("first/last val_loss:", history.history["val_loss"][0], history.history["val_loss"][-1])
print("best val_loss:", min(history.history["val_loss"]))

# ======================================================================
# EXTRA DEBUG: Why are predictions near-constant?
# Put this in the KERAS DEBUG SNAPSHOT cell, AFTER model is trained/loaded
# and AFTER train_inputs_tf/test_inputs_tf exist, BEFORE evaluation.
# ======================================================================

# ==========================================================
# Helper: quick stats (accepts np.ndarray OR tf.Tensor)
# ==========================================================
def _quick_stats(name, arr):
    if isinstance(arr, tf.Tensor):
        arr = arr.numpy()

    arr = np.asarray(arr)

    print(
        f"{name}: shape={arr.shape} dtype={arr.dtype} "
        f"min={np.nanmin(arr):.4f} "
        f"med={np.nanmedian(arr):.4f} "
        f"max={np.nanmax(arr):.4f} "
        f"std={np.nanstd(arr):.6f}"
    )


# ==========================================================
# Numeric input variance check
# ==========================================================
print("\n--- Numeric input variance check (raw numeric matrix) ---")

Xnum_test = test_inputs_tf["numeric"]  # tf.Tensor [N, D]

_quick_stats("test_inputs_tf['numeric']", Xnum_test)

# Per-column std (TF-native)
col_stds = tf.math.reduce_std(Xnum_test, axis=0)  # shape [D]

# Count near-constant columns
n_almost_const = int(
    tf.reduce_sum(tf.cast(col_stds < 1e-6, tf.int32)).numpy()
)

print(
    "Numeric cols with std < 1e-6:",
    n_almost_const,
    "/",
    int(Xnum_test.shape[1])
)

# Smallest 5 stds
smallest5 = tf.sort(col_stds)[:5].numpy()
print("Smallest 5 numeric stds:", smallest5)


# ==========================================================
# Categorical OOV rate check
# ==========================================================
print("\n--- Categorical OOV rate check (rebuild lookups and count OOV) ---")

oov_rates = {}
unique_id_counts = {}

# Sample rows for speed
N = len(X_train_nn)
rs = np.random.RandomState(42)
idx = rs.choice(N, size=min(20_000, N), replace=False)

for c in cat_cols_nn:
    lk = tf.keras.layers.StringLookup(
        output_mode="int",
        mask_token=None,
        num_oov_indices=1,
    )

    # IMPORTANT: must match model vocab exactly
    lk.set_vocabulary(LOOKUP_VOCABS[c])

    vals = X_train_nn[c].astype(str).to_numpy()[idx]
    ids = lk(vals).numpy()

    # With num_oov_indices=1, OOV bucket is index 0
    oov_rate = float((ids == 0).mean())

    oov_rates[c] = oov_rate
    unique_id_counts[c] = int(len(np.unique(ids)))

print("OOV rates (train sample):")
for c in cat_cols_nn:
    print(
        f"  {c:20s} "
        f"oov_rate={oov_rates[c]:.4f}  "
        f"unique_ids={unique_id_counts[c]}"
    )


# ==========================================================
# Prediction variance sanity check
# ==========================================================
print("\n--- Quick prediction variance on a small batch ---")

sample_inputs = {
    k: (v[:2048, :] if k == "numeric" else v[:2048])
    for k, v in test_inputs_tf.items()
}

pred_log_small = model.predict(
    sample_inputs,
    batch_size=256,
    verbose=0,
).ravel()

_quick_stats("pred_log_small", pred_log_small)

print("\n--- If pred_log_small std is still ~0, the model is collapsing ---")

print("EVAL model object:", model)
print("EVAL model name:", getattr(model, "name", None))
print("EVAL latest_path exists:", latest_path.exists())
print("EVAL KERAS_RUN_DIR:", KERAS_RUN_DIR)

# confirm head is the one you think
print("Has z_logit layer?", any(l.name == "z_logit" for l in model.layers))
print("Output layer name:", model.output_names)
print("Output tensor:", model.output)
```

#### Keras Evaluation 
```{python}
# ==========================================================
# Keras Model Evaluation
# ==========================================================
# ----------------------------
# Step 7: Evaluate (dollars) with full + clipped
# + Permutation importance (per-feature + optional subsample)
# + Training curves (if history exists)
# Ensures globals for metrics writer cell are set.
# ----------------------------

if ("model" not in globals()) or (model is None):
    print("⚠️ Keras model is not available — skipping evaluation.")
else:
    # ----------------------------
    # Helper: safe predict -> dollars
    # ----------------------------
    # def _predict_dollars(inputs: dict) -> np.ndarray:
    #     pred_log = model.predict(
    #         inputs,
    #         batch_size=KERAS_TUNE["pred_batch"],
    #         verbose=0
    #     ).ravel()

        # Safety: replace NaN/Inf if something went wrong
        # pred_log = np.nan_to_num(pred_log, nan=0.0, posinf=15.0, neginf=6.0)

        # # Convert to dollars in float64
        # return np.expm1(pred_log.astype("float64"))

    def _predict_dollars(inputs: dict) -> np.ndarray:
        """
        Predicts price in dollars from a dict of TF tensors / numpy arrays.

        This avoids Keras' model.predict() adapter path (which can trigger:
        "as_list() is not defined on an unknown TensorShape") by:
        1) converting inputs to TF tensors
        2) forcing static shapes for each input
        3) calling model(...) directly in eager mode
        """
        # numeric width must be known
        d_num = int(len(num_cols_nn)) if ("num_cols_nn" in globals()) else int(inputs["numeric"].shape[1])

        fixed = {}

        # numeric: (N, d_num) float32
        xnum = tf.convert_to_tensor(inputs["numeric"], dtype=tf.float32)
        if xnum.shape.rank != 2:
            xnum = tf.reshape(xnum, [-1, d_num])
        xnum.set_shape([None, d_num])
        fixed["numeric"] = xnum

        # # categoricals: (N, 1) tf.string
        # for k, v in inputs.items():
        #     if k == "numeric":
        #         continue

        #     x = tf.convert_to_tensor(v, dtype=tf.string)

        #     # ensure rank-2 column vector
        #     if x.shape.rank == 1:
        #         x = tf.expand_dims(x, axis=-1)   # (N,) -> (N,1)
        #     elif x.shape.rank == 2 and x.shape[-1] != 1:
        #         # if someone passed (N,) as (N,?) accidentally, coerce to (N,1)
        #         x = tf.reshape(x, [-1, 1])

        #     x.set_shape([None, 1])
        #     fixed[k] = x

        # categoricals: (N,) tf.string  (MATCH model input)
        for k, v in inputs.items():
            if k == "numeric":
                continue

            x = tf.convert_to_tensor(v, dtype=tf.string)

            # Ensure rank-1: (N,)
            if x.shape.rank == 2 and x.shape[-1] == 1:
                x = tf.reshape(x, [-1])
            elif x.shape.rank != 1:
                x = tf.reshape(x, [-1])

            x.set_shape([None])
            fixed[k] = x

        # Call model directly (avoid model.predict adapter)
        pred_log = model(fixed, training=False)              # (N,1)
        pred_log = tf.reshape(pred_log, [-1])               # (N,)

        # back to dollars (model outputs log1p(price))
        pred_dollars = tf.math.expm1(pred_log)

        return pred_dollars.numpy().astype("float64")

    # ----------------------------
    # TEST metrics (full + clipped)
    # ----------------------------
    true_test = np.expm1(y_test_nn.astype("float64").ravel())
    pred_test = _predict_dollars(test_inputs_tf)

    keras_test_m = metrics_full_and_clipped(true_test, pred_test, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99)
    display(metrics_to_df(keras_test_m, "Keras (Test)"))

    # ----------------------------
    # Train+Val metrics (full + clipped)
    # ----------------------------
    X_trainval_nn = pd.concat([X_train_nn, X_val_nn], axis=0)
    y_trainval_nn = np.vstack([y_train_nn, y_val_nn]) 
    trainval_inputs = to_input_dict(X_trainval_nn)

    true_trainval = np.expm1(y_trainval_nn.astype("float64").ravel())
    pred_trainval = _predict_dollars(trainval_inputs)

    keras_trainval_m = metrics_full_and_clipped(true_trainval, pred_trainval, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99)
    display(metrics_to_df(keras_trainval_m, "Keras (Train+Val)"))

    # Globals for your metrics
    keras_test_full = keras_test_m["full"]
    keras_test_clipped = keras_test_m["clipped"]
    keras_trainval_full = keras_trainval_m["full"]
    keras_trainval_clipped = keras_trainval_m["clipped"]

    # ----------------------------
    # Permutation importance (per-feature + optional subsample)
    # Robust for TF tensors + Keras predict (forces static shapes)
    # ----------------------------
    def permutation_importance_keras_per_feature(
        base_inputs: dict,
        y_true_dollars: np.ndarray,
        *,
        n_repeats: int = 3,
        seed: int = 42,
        max_rows: int | None = 20000,
        include_categoricals: bool = True,
    ):
        rs = np.random.RandomState(seed)

        # Cache expected widths for static shaping (based on the inputs we were given)
        d_num = int(base_inputs["numeric"].shape[1])  # must be known
        cat_keys = [k for k in base_inputs.keys() if k != "numeric"]

        def _force_static_shapes(inputs: dict) -> dict:
            """
            Ensure Keras sees the exact input ranks/shapes the model was built with:
            - numeric: (None, d_num) float32
            - each categorical: (None,) string   <-- IMPORTANT (no trailing 1 dim)
            """
            out = {}

            # -----------------------
            # numeric: (None, d_num)
            # -----------------------
            xnum = tf.convert_to_tensor(inputs["numeric"], dtype=tf.float32)

            # If someone accidentally passed numeric as (N,) or wrong last dim, coerce
            if xnum.shape.rank == 1:
                xnum = tf.reshape(xnum, [-1, d_num])
            elif xnum.shape.rank == 2 and xnum.shape[-1] != d_num:
                xnum = tf.reshape(xnum, [-1, d_num])

            xnum.set_shape([None, d_num])
            out["numeric"] = xnum

            # -----------------------
            # categoricals: (None,) string
            # -----------------------
            for k in cat_keys:
                x = tf.convert_to_tensor(inputs[k], dtype=tf.string)

                # If passed as (N,1), squeeze to (N,)
                if x.shape.rank == 2 and x.shape[-1] == 1:
                    x = tf.reshape(x, [-1])
                # If any other unexpected rank, coerce to (N,)
                elif x.shape.rank != 1:
                    x = tf.reshape(x, [-1])

                x.set_shape([None])
                out[k] = x

            return out

        def _gather_rows_tf(inputs: dict, idx_rows: np.ndarray) -> dict:
            idx_tf = tf.convert_to_tensor(idx_rows, dtype=tf.int32)
            out = {}
            for k, v in inputs.items():
                v = tf.convert_to_tensor(v)
                out[k] = tf.gather(v, idx_tf, axis=0)
            return _force_static_shapes(out)

        # ----------------------------
        # 0) Subsample rows for speed (optional)
        # ----------------------------
        N = int(len(y_true_dollars))
        if (max_rows is not None) and (N > int(max_rows)):
            idx_rows = rs.choice(N, size=int(max_rows), replace=False)
            sub_inputs = _gather_rows_tf(base_inputs, idx_rows)
            y_sub = y_true_dollars[idx_rows]
        else:
            sub_inputs = _force_static_shapes(base_inputs)
            y_sub = y_true_dollars

        # ----------------------------
        # 1) Baseline RMSE
        # ----------------------------
        base_pred = _predict_dollars(sub_inputs)
        base_rmse = float(np.sqrt(np.mean((y_sub - base_pred) ** 2)))

        rows = []

        # ----------------------------
        # 2) Numeric per-column importance
        # ----------------------------
        Xnum = sub_inputs["numeric"]  # (N, d_num)
        n_sub = int(Xnum.shape[0]) if Xnum.shape[0] is not None else len(y_sub)

        if ("num_cols_nn" in globals()) and (len(num_cols_nn) == d_num):
            num_names = list(num_cols_nn)
        else:
            num_names = [f"num_{j}" for j in range(d_num)]

        for j in range(d_num):
            deltas = []
            for _ in range(int(n_repeats)):
                idx_perm = rs.permutation(len(y_sub))
                idx_tf = tf.convert_to_tensor(idx_perm, dtype=tf.int32)

                col_perm = tf.gather(Xnum[:, j], idx_tf)              # (N,)
                col_perm = tf.expand_dims(col_perm, axis=1)           # (N,1)

                left  = Xnum[:, :j]                                   # (N, j)
                right = Xnum[:, j+1:]                                 # (N, d-j-1)
                Xnum_new = tf.concat([left, col_perm, right], axis=1) # (N, d_num)
                Xnum_new.set_shape([None, d_num])

                pert = dict(sub_inputs)
                pert["numeric"] = Xnum_new
                pert = _force_static_shapes(pert)

                pred = _predict_dollars(pert)
                rmse = float(np.sqrt(np.mean((y_sub - pred) ** 2)))
                deltas.append(rmse - base_rmse)

            rows.append({
                "feature": num_names[j],
                "feature_type": "numeric",
                "rmse_increase": float(np.mean(deltas)),
                "rmse_increase_std": float(np.std(deltas)),
            })

        # ----------------------------
        # 3) Categorical per-key importance (optional)
        # ----------------------------
        if include_categoricals:
            for key in cat_keys:
                deltas = []
                Xcat = sub_inputs[key]  # (N,) tf.string   <-- FIXED

                for _ in range(int(n_repeats)):
                    idx_perm = rs.permutation(len(y_sub))
                    idx_tf = tf.convert_to_tensor(idx_perm, dtype=tf.int32)

                    Xcat_new = tf.gather(Xcat, idx_tf, axis=0)  # (N,)
                    Xcat_new.set_shape([None])                  # <-- FIXED (no [None,1])

                    pert = dict(sub_inputs)
                    pert[key] = Xcat_new
                    pert = _force_static_shapes(pert)

                    pred = _predict_dollars(pert)
                    rmse = float(np.sqrt(np.mean((y_sub - pred) ** 2)))
                    deltas.append(rmse - base_rmse)

                rows.append({
                    "feature": key,
                    "feature_type": "categorical",
                    "rmse_increase": float(np.mean(deltas)),
                    "rmse_increase_std": float(np.std(deltas)),
                })

        df_pi = (
            pd.DataFrame(rows)
            .sort_values("rmse_increase", ascending=False)
            .reset_index(drop=True)
        )
        return base_rmse, df_pi

    # ---- Run it ----
    try:
        base_rmse_pi, pi_df = permutation_importance_keras_per_feature(
            test_inputs_tf,
            true_test,  # must be dollars
            n_repeats=3,
            seed=SEED if "SEED" in globals() else 42,
            max_rows=20000,
            include_categoricals=True
        )
        print(f"Baseline TEST RMSE ($) [subset for PI]: {base_rmse_pi:,.0f}")
        display(pi_df.head(30))
    except Exception as e:
        print("⚠️ Permutation importance failed:", e)

    # ----------------------------
    # Training curves (if this run trained in-session)
    # ----------------------------
    if ("history" in globals()) and (history is not None):
        hist = history.history if hasattr(history, "history") else {}
        if ("loss" in hist) or ("val_loss" in hist):
            plt.figure(figsize=(7, 4))
            if "loss" in hist:
                plt.plot(hist["loss"], label="loss")
            if "val_loss" in hist:
                plt.plot(hist["val_loss"], label="val_loss")
            plt.xlabel("Epoch")
            plt.ylabel("Loss")
            plt.title("Keras Training Curves")
            plt.legend()
            plt.tight_layout()
            plt.show()
        else:
            print("ℹ️ history exists but does not contain loss/val_loss keys.")
    else:
        print("ℹ️ No training history available (likely loaded model or training skipped).")

    print("✅ Done: Keras NN tuned/trained and evaluated (with permutation importance + curves).")
```

### Metrics Savepoint
```{python}
# -------------------------
# Save Metrics
# Standard schema across models:
#   metrics[model]["trainval"]["full"/"clipped"]
#   metrics[model]["test"]["full"/"clipped"]
# -------------------------

metrics = {}

# ---- Meta ----
metrics["_meta"] = {
    "timestamp": datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),
    "train_mode": TRAIN_MODE if "TRAIN_MODE" in globals() else None,
    "updates_latest": (TRAIN_MODE == "full") if "TRAIN_MODE" in globals() else None,
    "rows": {
        "train": int(len(X_train)) if "X_train" in globals() else None,
        "val": int(len(X_val)) if "X_val" in globals() else None,
        "test": int(len(X_test)) if "X_test" in globals() else None,
        "total": int(len(X)) if "X" in globals() else None,
    },
    "clip_policy": {
        "basis": "y_true_quantiles",
        "q_low": 0.01,
        "q_high": 0.99,
        "note": (
            "Clipped metrics computed on subsets where true price is within "
            "[q_low, q_high] quantiles; predictions are never used for clipping."
        ),
    },
}

# Helper: safe string-or-None
def _str_or_none(x):
    return str(x) if x is not None else None

# Helper: build a split metrics object from globals
def _split_payload(full_key: str, clipped_key: str):
    full = globals().get(full_key, None)
    clipped = globals().get(clipped_key, None)
    return {
        "full": full,
        "clipped": clipped,
    }

# -------------------------
# Ridge metrics
# Requires you to set these in Ridge eval:
#   ridge_test_full, ridge_test_clipped
#   ridge_trainval_full, ridge_trainval_clipped
# -------------------------
if all(k in globals() for k in ["ridge_test_full", "ridge_test_clipped",
                                "ridge_trainval_full", "ridge_trainval_clipped"]):
    metrics["ridge"] = {
        "trainval": _split_payload("ridge_trainval_full", "ridge_trainval_clipped"),
        "test": _split_payload("ridge_test_full", "ridge_test_clipped"),
    }
else:
    print("ℹ️ Skipping Ridge metrics write (need ridge_*_full and ridge_*_clipped).")

# -------------------------
# CatBoost metrics
# Requires you to set these in CatBoost eval:
#   cb_test_full, cb_test_clipped
#   cb_trainval_full, cb_trainval_clipped
#   cb_train_full/cb_train_clipped, cb_val_full/cb_val_clipped
# -------------------------
if all(k in globals() for k in ["cb_test_full", "cb_test_clipped",
                                "cb_trainval_full", "cb_trainval_clipped"]):
    metrics["catboost"] = {
        "trainval": _split_payload("cb_trainval_full", "cb_trainval_clipped"),
        "test": _split_payload("cb_test_full", "cb_test_clipped"),
        "best_iteration": (
            int(final_cb.get_best_iteration())
            if ("final_cb" in globals() and final_cb is not None)
            else None
        ),
        "run_dir": _str_or_none(globals().get("RUN_DIR", None)),
    }

    # CV-best val metrics
    if all(name in globals() for name in ["mae_cv", "rmse_cv"]):
        metrics["catboost"]["val_cvbest"] = {
            "mae": float(mae_cv),
            "rmse": float(rmse_cv),
        }

    # Store explicit train/val split metrics if you created them
    if all(k in globals() for k in ["cb_train_full", "cb_train_clipped"]):
        metrics["catboost"]["train"] = _split_payload("cb_train_full", "cb_train_clipped")

    if all(k in globals() for k in ["cb_val_full", "cb_val_clipped"]):
        metrics["catboost"]["val"] = _split_payload("cb_val_full", "cb_val_clipped")

else:
    print("ℹ️ Skipping CatBoost metrics write (need cb_*_full and cb_*_clipped).")

# -------------------------
# Keras metrics
# Requires you to set these in Keras eval:
#   keras_test_full, keras_test_clipped
#   keras_trainval_full, keras_trainval_clipped
# -------------------------
if all(k in globals() for k in ["keras_test_full", "keras_test_clipped",
                                "keras_trainval_full", "keras_trainval_clipped"]):
    metrics["keras"] = {
        "trainval": _split_payload("keras_trainval_full", "keras_trainval_clipped"),
        "test": _split_payload("keras_test_full", "keras_test_clipped"),
        "run_dir": _str_or_none(globals().get("KERAS_RUN_DIR", None)),
    }
else:
    print("ℹ️ Skipping Keras metrics write (need keras_*_full and keras_*_clipped).")

# -------------------------
# Write files
# -------------------------
metrics_ts = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
METRICS_VERSIONED_PATH = METRICS_DIR / f"metrics_{metrics_ts}.json"

atomic_write_json(METRICS_PATH, metrics)
print("🧾 Wrote metrics:", METRICS_PATH)

atomic_write_json(METRICS_VERSIONED_PATH, metrics)
print("🧾 Wrote versioned metrics:", METRICS_VERSIONED_PATH)

# Run-specific metrics (CatBoost run dir)
if ("RUN_DIR" in globals()) and (globals().get("RUN_DIR", None) is not None):
    atomic_write_json(globals()["RUN_DIR"] / "metrics.json", metrics)
    print("🧾 Wrote run metrics:", globals()["RUN_DIR"] / "metrics.json")

# Run-specific metrics (Keras run dir)
if ("KERAS_RUN_DIR" in globals()) and (globals().get("KERAS_RUN_DIR", None) is not None):
    keras_only = {
        "_meta": metrics["_meta"],
        "keras": metrics.get("keras", None),
    }
    atomic_write_json(globals()["KERAS_RUN_DIR"] / "keras_metrics.json", keras_only)
    print("🧾 Wrote Keras-only metrics:", globals()["KERAS_RUN_DIR"] / "keras_metrics.json")

```

## Model Comparison Graphs

### Plotting Functions for Models
```{python}
# =========================
# Plot Function 1
# =========================

def clip_by_quantile(y_true, y_pred, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99):
    y_true = np.asarray(y_true)
    y_pred = np.asarray(y_pred)
    lo, hi = np.quantile(y_true, [q_low, q_high])
    mask = (y_true >= lo) & (y_true <= hi)
    return y_true[mask], y_pred[mask], mask, (lo, hi)

# =========================
# Plot Function 2
# =========================

def plot_regression_diagnostics(
    y_true, y_pred,
    X_ref=None, feature_for_residual="age",
    clip=False, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99,
    title_prefix=""
):
    """
    Plots:
      1) Predicted vs Actual
      2) Residuals vs Predicted
      3) Residuals vs a chosen feature (e.g. age or mileage)
      
    Clips by y_true quantiles for readability for some charts.
    """
    y_true = np.asarray(y_true)
    y_pred = np.asarray(y_pred)

    if clip:
        y_true_p, y_pred_p, mask, (lo, hi) = clip_by_quantile(y_true, y_pred, q_low, q_high)
        label_suffix = f" (clipped {int(q_low*100)}–{int(q_high*100)}% of y_true)"
    else:
        y_true_p, y_pred_p = y_true, y_pred
        mask = None
        label_suffix = " (unclipped)"

    # 1) Predicted vs Actual
    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 6))
    plt.scatter(y_true_p, y_pred_p, alpha=0.2)
    mn = min(y_true_p.min(), y_pred_p.min())
    mx = max(y_true_p.max(), y_pred_p.max())
    plt.plot([mn, mx], [mn, mx], "r--")
    plt.xlabel("Actual Price ($)")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Price ($)")
    plt.title(f"{title_prefix} Predicted vs Actual{label_suffix}")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

    residuals = y_true_p - y_pred_p

    # 2) Residuals vs Predicted
    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
    plt.scatter(y_pred_p, residuals, alpha=0.2)
    plt.axhline(0, color="red", linestyle="--")
    plt.xlabel("Predicted Price ($)")
    plt.ylabel("Residual ($)  (actual - predicted)")
    plt.title(f"{title_prefix} Residuals vs Predicted{label_suffix}")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

    # 3) Residuals vs Feature
    if X_ref is not None and feature_for_residual in X_ref.columns:
        if mask is not None:
            # mask aligns to y_true/y_pred order; apply same mask to X_ref
            x_feat = np.asarray(X_ref[feature_for_residual])[mask]
        else:
            x_feat = np.asarray(X_ref[feature_for_residual])

        plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
        plt.scatter(x_feat, residuals, alpha=0.2)
        plt.axhline(0, color="red", linestyle="--")
        plt.xlabel(feature_for_residual)
        plt.ylabel("Residual ($)")
        plt.title(f"{title_prefix} Residuals vs {feature_for_residual}{label_suffix}")
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.show()

# =========================
# Plot Function 3
# =========================

def plot_abs_error_vs_actual(
    y_true, y_pred,
    clip=False, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99,
    title_prefix="",
    log_x=True
):
    """
    Plot absolute error vs actual price.
    Clip based on y_true quantiles for readability (optional).
    Log-scale the x-axis (actual price).
    """
    y_true = np.asarray(y_true)
    y_pred = np.asarray(y_pred)

    if clip:
        y_true_p, y_pred_p, _, _ = clip_by_quantile(y_true, y_pred, q_low, q_high)
        label_suffix = f" (clipped {int(q_low*100)}–{int(q_high*100)}% of y_true)"
    else:
        y_true_p, y_pred_p = y_true, y_pred
        label_suffix = " (unclipped)"

    abs_err = np.abs(y_true_p - y_pred_p)

    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
    plt.scatter(y_true_p, abs_err, alpha=0.2)
    plt.ylabel("Absolute Error ($)")
    plt.title(f"{title_prefix} Absolute Error vs Actual{label_suffix}")
    if log_x:
        plt.xscale("log")
        plt.xlabel("Actual Price ($) [log scale]")
    else:
        plt.xlabel("Actual Price ($)")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

# =========================
# Plot Function 4
# =========================

def plot_pred_vs_pred(
    pred_a, pred_b,
    *,
    name_a="Model A",
    name_b="Model B",
    clip_by_y_true=None,   # pass y_true array to clip both preds by y_true quantiles
    clip=False,
    q_low=0.01,
    q_high=0.99
):
    """
    Scatter of predictions from two models against each other.
    Clipping is based on y_true quantiles (optional).
    """
    pred_a = np.asarray(pred_a)
    pred_b = np.asarray(pred_b)

    if clip:
        if clip_by_y_true is None:
            raise ValueError("clip=True requires clip_by_y_true (the y_true array).")
        y_true = np.asarray(clip_by_y_true)
        _, _, mask, _ = clip_by_quantile(y_true, pred_a, q_low=q_low, q_high=q_high)
        pred_a_p = pred_a[mask]
        pred_b_p = pred_b[mask]
        suffix = f" (clipped {int(q_low*100)}–{int(q_high*100)}% of y_true)"
    else:
        pred_a_p, pred_b_p = pred_a, pred_b
        suffix = " (unclipped)"

    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 6))
    plt.scatter(pred_a_p, pred_b_p, alpha=0.2)
    mn = min(pred_a_p.min(), pred_b_p.min())
    mx = max(pred_a_p.max(), pred_b_p.max())
    plt.plot([mn, mx], [mn, mx], "r--")
    plt.xlabel(f"{name_a} Predicted Price ($)")
    plt.ylabel(f"{name_b} Predicted Price ($)")
    plt.title(f"{name_a} vs {name_b} Predictions{suffix}")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

### Model Graph Section
```{python}
# =========================
# Model Comparison Plots -- using test set
# =========================

if ("X_test" not in globals()) or ("y_test" not in globals()):
    print("⚠️ X_test/y_test not found — cannot plot comparisons.")
else:
    y_true_test = np.expm1(y_test)

    # Choose feature for residual-vs-feature
    feature_name = "age" if "age" in X_test.columns else ("mileage" if "mileage" in X_test.columns else None)

    # ----------------------------------------------------------
    # Collect predictions from any models that exist
    # ----------------------------------------------------------
    preds = {}

    if ("best_ridge" in globals()) and (best_ridge is not None):
        preds["Ridge"] = np.expm1(best_ridge.predict(X_test))
    else:
        print("ℹ️ Ridge not available — skipping Ridge plots.")

    if ("final_cb" in globals()) and (final_cb is not None):
        preds["CatBoost"] = np.expm1(final_cb.predict(X_test))
    else:
        print("ℹ️ CatBoost not available — skipping CatBoost plots.")

    # if ("model" in globals()) and (model is not None) and ("test_inputs" in globals()):
    #     y_pred_log_keras = model.predict(test_inputs, batch_size=4096, verbose=0).ravel()  # log1p preds
    #     preds["Keras"] = np.expm1(y_pred_log_keras)  # dollars
    # else:
    #     print("ℹ️ Keras model/test_inputs not available — skipping Keras plots.")

    if ("model" in globals()) and (model is not None) and (("test_inputs_tf" in globals()) or ("test_inputs" in globals())):
        # Prefer tf.data pipeline if available (static shapes + correct string ranks)
        if ("test_inputs_tf" in globals()) and (test_inputs_tf is not None):
            preds["Keras"] = _predict_dollars(test_inputs_tf)
        else:
            # fallback (older path) if you still have a numpy/dict input
            preds["Keras"] = _predict_dollars(test_inputs)
    else:
        print("ℹ️ Keras model/test_inputs not available — skipping Keras plots.")


    if len(preds) == 0:
        print("⚠️ No model predictions available — nothing to plot.")
    else:
        # ----------------------------------------------------------
        # 3 diagnostic plots + abs error plot for each model
        # (unclipped + clipped)
        # ----------------------------------------------------------
        for name, y_pred in preds.items():
            plot_regression_diagnostics(
                y_true_test, y_pred,
                X_ref=X_test,
                feature_for_residual=(feature_name or "age"),
                clip=False,
                title_prefix=f"{name} |"
            )
            plot_regression_diagnostics(
                y_true_test, y_pred,
                X_ref=X_test,
                feature_for_residual=(feature_name or "age"),
                clip=True, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99,
                title_prefix=f"{name} |"
            )

            plot_abs_error_vs_actual(
                y_true_test, y_pred,
                clip=False,
                title_prefix=f"{name} |",
                log_x=True
            )
            plot_abs_error_vs_actual(
                y_true_test, y_pred,
                clip=True, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99,
                title_prefix=f"{name} |",
                log_x=True
            )

        # ----------------------------------------------------------
        # 4th plot: pred vs pred for every pair of models
        # (unclipped + clipped)
        # ----------------------------------------------------------
        model_names = list(preds.keys())
        if len(model_names) >= 2:
            for i in range(len(model_names)):
                for j in range(i + 1, len(model_names)):
                    a = model_names[i]
                    b = model_names[j]

                    plot_pred_vs_pred(
                        preds[a], preds[b],
                        name_a=a, name_b=b,
                        clip=False
                    )
                    plot_pred_vs_pred(
                        preds[a], preds[b],
                        name_a=a, name_b=b,
                        clip_by_y_true=y_true_test,
                        clip=True, q_low=0.01, q_high=0.99
                    )
        else:
            print("ℹ️ Only one model available — skipping pred-vs-pred comparison plots.")
```
